_dataset_notice: CURATED SEED DATASET: Entries are real US programs from identifiable sponsors, including national awards and a small school-specific pilot tied to official university pages. Every field must be independently verified before production use. Fields marked VERIFY are intentionally incomplete placeholders. Do not show VERIFY values to end users without review.

scholarships.name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
scholarships.description: Merit-based scholarship for high school seniors planning to pursue postsecondary education. Recognizes leadership, service, and academic achievement.

verification.notes: Apply page: $20,000 award, 3.0 GPA minimum, Phase 1 open Aug 3–Sept 30 (2027 cohort opens Aug 3, 2026), U.S. citizen/national/PR or eligible federal-aid statuses; Phase 1 requires no essays. Application checklist verified 2026-06-24 from official apply page (Phase 1 Aug 3–Sep 30).

application_requirements.label: Complete Phase 1 online application
application_requirements.details: Phase 1 opens Aug 3 and closes Sep 30 each year for students graduating the following academic year. No essays, transcript, or recommendations in Phase 1.

application_requirements.label: Confirm eligibility before applying
application_requirements.details: U.S. high school senior; U.S. citizen/national/permanent resident or eligible federal-aid statuses; minimum 3.0 GPA; planning accredited U.S. post-secondary study.

application_requirements.label: Submit Phase 2 materials if named Semifinalist
application_requirements.details: About 1% of applicants are named Semifinalists by end of October and invited to complete Phase 2 in November, including essays, transcript, and one recommendation.

application_requirements.label: Complete regional finalist interview if invited
application_requirements.details: From Semifinalists, 250 advance as Regional Finalists and participate in a final interview with alumni/staff in late January or early February.

scholarships.name: The Gates Scholarship
scholarships.description: Highly selective last-dollar scholarship for outstanding high school seniors from low-income households. Covers full cost of attendance not already met by financial aid. Applicants must be Pell-eligible.

verification.notes: Scholarship page: last-dollar full COA award, 3.3 weighted GPA minimum, U.S. citizen or permanent resident, Pell-eligible senior; 2025 cycle closed Sept 15 — next deadline not yet published. Checklist verified 2026-06-24 from official pages; next-cycle application component list not published outside myTGS.

application_requirements.label: Create a myTGS account and apply
application_requirements.details: Apply through the myTGS portal on the official site. Detailed upload requirements are inside the application system.

application_requirements.label: Meet basic eligibility
application_requirements.details: High school senior; Pell-eligible; U.S. citizen or permanent resident; minimum 3.3 cumulative weighted GPA; plan full-time enrollment at accredited not-for-profit U.S. four-year college.

application_requirements.label: Submit the FAFSA
application_requirements.details: Official site requires FAFSA submission. The award is last-dollar based on cost of attendance not covered by other aid and your Student Aid Index.

scholarships.name: Jack Kent Cooke Foundation College Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Supports high-achieving high school seniors with financial need who complete four years of high school in the U.S., a U.S. territory, or on a U.S. military base and seek to attend a four-year college.

verification.notes: Official eligibility FAQ lists senior standing after four years of high school in the U.S., a U.S. territory, or on a U.S. military base; fall 2027 four-year college enrollment; 3.75 unweighted GPA; test score submission; and unmet financial need. No citizenship requirement is listed. Application checklist verified 2026-06-24 from JKCF how-to-apply page.

application_requirements.label: Apply via Common App and add JKCF
application_requirements.details: Submit through Common App, add Jack Kent Cooke Foundation as a school to access program questions. Opens Aug 19, 2026; deadline Nov 11, 2026.

application_requirements.label: Two academic recommendations
application_requirements.details: One from an 11th-grade core subject teacher and one from another core teacher/professor since 9th grade, both submitted through Common App.

application_requirements.label: Counselor recommendation and School Report
application_requirements.details: One counselor recommendation and School Report/School Profile submitted through Common App Recommenders and FERPA.

application_requirements.label: Unofficial transcripts and test scores
application_requirements.details: Self-report courses/grades and submit unofficial transcripts in Common App. Submit SAT, ACT, AP, or IB score(s); no minimum score but a test must have been taken.

application_requirements.label: JKCF essays and financial information
application_requirements.details: Complete JKCF-specific questions including short essays and financial information demonstrating unmet need.

scholarships.name: Dell Scholars Program
scholarships.description: College readiness and completion program for students who demonstrate grit, potential, and financial need. Includes scholarship funds and individualized support.

verification.notes: Official students page: $20,000 plus laptop and textbook credits, 2.4 GPA minimum, Pell Grant eligible senior in approved college-readiness program; 2025-26 cycle opened Dec 15 2025 and closed Feb 15 2026. Next-cycle open/close dates not yet published.

application_requirements.label: Complete online application
application_requirements.details: All applications are submitted online only. Official Important Dates section lists December 15 open and February 15 final deadline for the published cycle.

application_requirements.label: Self-report high school coursework and grades
application_requirements.details: Initial application requires manually reporting high school coursework and grades with status for each course (In Progress, Completed, or Not Taken).

application_requirements.label: Finalist materials: transcript, FAFSA Summary, recommendation
application_requirements.details: If selected as a finalist, upload current high school transcript, FAFSA Submission Summary, and provide one online recommendation.

scholarships.name: Horatio Alger National Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Horatio Alger Association of Distinguished Americans
scholarships.description: 105 national awards of $25,000 for high school juniors who have overcome adversity, demonstrate financial need (AGI $100,000 or lower), and plan to pursue a bachelor's degree.

verification.notes: Undergraduate scholarships page: 105×$25,000 National Scholarship, 2.0 GPA minimum, U.S. citizen, junior standing; toolkit shows junior application closes March 1 — next cycle not yet published. Application checklist verified 2026-06-24 from Horatio Alger undergraduate scholarships page.

application_requirements.label: Complete one online undergraduate application
application_requirements.details: One application considers you for all eligible programs including the National Scholarship.

application_requirements.label: Craft essay responses
application_requirements.details: Essays include your Horatio Alger story and American Dream experience per official how-to-apply steps.

application_requirements.label: Counselor Certification Form
application_requirements.details: Enter counselor email so counselor receives a request to submit the Counselor Certification Form verifying academic information.

application_requirements.label: Support Form Provider
application_requirements.details: Enter email for an unrelated adult 21+ familiar with adversities described in your short responses.

application_requirements.label: Upload transcript and income verification
application_requirements.details: Upload high school transcript including fall grades and income verification (most often parents' tax form) through the online application.

scholarships.name: Davidson Fellows Scholarship
scholarships.description: Recognizes students 18 or younger who have completed significant projects in science, technology, engineering, mathematics, literature, music, philosophy, or outside the box.

verification.notes: Eligibility page: $100k/$50k/$25k awards, age 18 or under (no grade minimum), U.S. citizen or PR; 2026 deadline was Feb 18 — 2027 application opens fall 2026.

application_requirements.label: Submit part one: a brief description of your project or portfolio
application_requirements.details: Part one screens eligibility and is held for review for 1-2 business days. Submit it as early as possible: you cannot start part two until it is approved, and approval is what gives you and your nominators time to finish. Applying as a 2-person team? Email DavidsonFellows@DavidsonGifted.org BEFORE starting.

application_requirements.label: Submit part two: your category requirements
application_requirements.details: Categories are Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics, Literature, Music, Philosophy, and Outside the Box; each has its own requirements. Community service projects are NOT accepted, and describing a future major or occupation is not an eligible submission. Google products, including Google Docs and videos, are not accepted; any YouTube videos must be set to Unlisted.

application_requirements.label: Send your nominators their nominating forms
application_requirements.details: You enter your nominators' information in part two and the system sends them their forms. Read the sponsor's SAMPLE Nominating Form first so you can choose the best nominators to support your submission.

application_requirements.label: Send your parent their approval form
application_requirements.details: Only one parent approval form is required. For a team submission, one parent form is required per team member.

scholarships.name: Regeneron Science Talent Search
scholarships.description: Premier science and math competition for high school seniors conducting original research. Students submit research reports and compete for awards at the national level.

verification.notes: Official FAQ says any student living in and attending their last year of secondary school in the U.S. and its territories may apply regardless of citizenship; U.S. citizens living abroad may also qualify, while non-citizens at American schools abroad are not eligible. Awards up to $250k; application due Nov 5 2026 8pm ET; no minimum GPA. Application checklist verified 2026-06-24 from Regeneron STS application requirements page.

application_requirements.label: Original research report
application_requirements.details: Submit a research report describing an original independent research project meeting eligibility requirements.

application_requirements.label: Three recommendations
application_requirements.details: Educator recommendation, project recommendation, and High School Report (transcript) required.

application_requirements.label: Complete application sections
application_requirements.details: Sections cover you, your research project, prior research, activities, and how you spend your time.

application_requirements.label: Short essays
application_requirements.details: Short essay section required as part of holistic application review.

scholarships.name: National Merit Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Academic competition entered by taking the PSAT/NMSQT in the designated year of high school. Semifinalists and finalists compete for National Merit and corporate-sponsored scholarships.

verification.notes: Official program guide: entry via PSAT/NMSQT in junior year; $2,500 National Merit awards; semifinalists submit personal essay; no GPA minimum; citizenship required only for students at schools outside the U.S.

scholarships.name: Burger King Scholars Program
scholarships.description: Merit-based awards for high school seniors, Burger King employees, and their families who plan to enroll in accredited two- or four-year colleges.

verification.notes: Official Scholarship America guidelines: General Track $1k-$60k, 2.0 GPA minimum for seniors, U.S./PR/Guam/Canada residency; application was complete only if submitted electronically by Dec 15 2025 or before the 30,000-application cap. Citizenship not stated for General Track.

application_requirements.label: Register a student account and complete your student profile
application_requirements.details: Apply early: submissions are capped at 30,000 and access to the application closes once that number is reached, whether or not the deadline has passed. The 2026 program's dates are tentative; the last cycle ran October 15 to December 15. Registration uses an email validation step, so use a valid address.

application_requirements.label: Upload a current, complete transcript of grades
application_requirements.details: High school or college transcript. Grade reports are NOT accepted. An unofficial or online transcript must display your name, the school name, grades, and credit hours for each course and term.

application_requirements.label: Gather your work experience and activities list
application_requirements.details: A list of your work experiences and involvement in school and community activities. Awards weigh GPA, work experience, extracurricular activities, community service, and financial need.

application_requirements.label: Gather your family's financial information
application_requirements.details: The sponsor suggests having a copy of your parents' most recent tax form filed with the IRS or Revenue Canada on hand when filling out that section.

application_requirements.label: Add Burger King employee information
application_requirements.details: Only if your application is to be affiliated with a Burger King employee (the Employee-Based Track).

scholarships.name: Elks Most Valuable Student Scholarship
scholarships.description: Four-year scholarships for high school seniors based on scholarship, leadership, and financial need. Funded by the Elks National Foundation.

verification.notes: MVS page: $4k–$30k four-year awards, U.S. citizen only (no permanent residents), senior; 2027 application opens Aug 1 2026 — deadline not yet published (2026 cycle was Nov 12). Checklist verified 2026-06-24 from ENF sample application PDF; 2027 exact deadlines not yet on main MVS page.

application_requirements.label: Complete online MVS application fields
application_requirements.details: Complete all required fields on the online application form and click Submit. Initial application requires no supporting documents.

application_requirements.label: Request Counselor Report
application_requirements.details: Request a Counselor Report from your counselor using name and email on the counselor page. Incomplete applications are not considered.

application_requirements.label: Upload transcript if named Quarter Finalist
application_requirements.details: If advanced to Quarter Finalist, upload high school transcript (PDF). Semi-Finalists later provide FAFSA Submission Summary.

scholarships.name: Ron Brown Scholar Program
scholarships.description: Scholarship and leadership development for academically talented African American high school seniors with demonstrated leadership, community service, and financial need.

verification.notes: Apply page: 20×$40,000 ($10k/yr), Black/African American senior, U.S. citizen or permanent resident, two essays; opens after Sept 3, deadline Dec 1.

scholarships.name: Hispanic Scholarship Fund Scholar Program
scholarships.description: Scholarships for students of Hispanic heritage pursuing higher education. Open to high school seniors and current college students.

verification.notes: Scholarship page: $500–$5k awards, 3.0 GPA (HS) / 2.5 (college), U.S. citizen/PR/DACA, Hispanic heritage, FAFSA required; 2026 cycle closed Feb 15 — next deadline not yet published. Checklist verified 2026-06-24 from HSF scholarship page; detailed upload list is inside myHSF portal.

application_requirements.label: Complete myHSF Scholar application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the myHSF profile/application portal on the official site.

application_requirements.label: Submit the FAFSA
application_requirements.details: Complete and submit the FAFSA for the applicable aid year as an eligibility requirement listed on the official scholarship page.

scholarships.name: QuestBridge National College Match
scholarships.award_amount: full four-year scholarship to partner college (varies by school)
scholarships.description: Connects high-achieving, low-income high school seniors with full four-year scholarships at QuestBridge partner colleges through a national matching process.

verification.notes: Official QuestBridge apply page: open to any high school senior currently attending high school in the U.S. plus U.S. citizens and permanent residents living abroad; international students living outside the U.S. are not eligible. Dates page confirms Oct 1 application deadline.

application_requirements.label: Complete National College Match application
application_requirements.details: Provide academics, activities, household/financial background, writing section, two core-subject teacher recommendations, transcript(s), counselor School Report, and test scores if taken.

application_requirements.label: Gather financial documents
application_requirements.details: Have prior-year tax returns, W-2(s), or other financial documents ready to complete the application accurately.

application_requirements.label: Rank up to 15 college partners
application_requirements.details: After submitting the application, rank up to 15 college partners via the Match Rankings Form for early admission and full four-year scholarship consideration.

application_requirements.label: Submit signed Match Agreement Form
application_requirements.details: Due October 15 after the Match Rankings Form. Must be signed by applicant, parent/guardian, and school counselor and uploaded to your Manage page.

application_requirements.label: Submit Match Requirements if named Finalist
application_requirements.details: Finalists must submit additional requirements to each ranked college. Official 2026 timeline lists Match Requirements deadline November 1.

scholarships.name: Foot Locker Scholar Athletes Program
scholarships.description: Honors student-athletes who demonstrate exceptional academic ability, strong leadership skills, and passionate community involvement.

verification.notes: Official sponsor URL recorded on June 21, 2026. On 2026-07-02, the official URL failed DNS resolution and no current official application page was found; deadline is left unknown for human review.

scholarships.name: Cameron Impact Scholarship
scholarships.award_amount: full tuition and qualified educational expenses
scholarships.description: Merit-based scholarship for high school seniors who demonstrate excellence in academics, extracurricular activities, leadership, and community service.

verification.notes: FAQ: 15 awards, full tuition and qualified educational expenses, 3.7 unweighted GPA, U.S. citizen; Class of 2027 closed May 1 2026 — next cycle deadline not yet published. Application checklist verified 2026-06-24 from Bryan Cameron scholarship and FAQ pages.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online application
application_requirements.details: Submit the online application by the deadline. Program accepts up to 3,000 complete eligible applications or closes when the published deadline passes, whichever occurs first.

application_requirements.label: Two recommendation letters
application_requirements.details: One from current school-affiliated personnel; second from someone who has made a significant impact on your life. Parent/guardian letters are not accepted.

application_requirements.label: Submit transcript
application_requirements.details: Transcript certifying academic performance required; unofficial copy acceptable. May upload in the application or email the foundation.

scholarships.name: APIA Scholars Program
scholarships.sponsor: Asian & Pacific Islander American Scholarship Fund
scholarships.description: Scholarships for undergraduate students with financial need who are enrolling or continuing at accredited U.S. postsecondary institutions.

verification.notes: Scholarship page: $2,500–$20,000, no GPA minimum (removed), citizen/national/PR or Pacific compact states, undergrad; 2027-2028 cycle opens Nov 15 2026, closes Jan 15 2027 5pm EST. Application checklist verified 2026-06-24 from APIA application guide.

application_requirements.label: Complete online APIA Scholarship application
application_requirements.details: One application covers all APIA scholarships. Initial application includes one 500-word essay and does not require recommendation letters or supporting documents.

application_requirements.label: Required essay
application_requirements.details: Answer the published two-part essay prompt about experiences, identity, community, and future goals. Maximum 500 words.

items.prompt: How have your experiences, identity, or community shaped the way you interact with the world? How would the APIA Scholarship support your continued growth as you pursue your future goals? This is a two-part question. Please ensure that you answer every part of the question. Be specific and provide examples.

application_requirements.label: Provide FAFSA dependency information
application_requirements.details: Application asks dependency status for the FAFSA. APIA does not require FAFSA submission at the initial application stage per the official guide.

application_requirements.label: Additional materials if advanced
application_requirements.details: Applicants advancing to later selection stages may be asked for additional forms. Recipients later submit transcript and financial information.

scholarships.name: POSSE Foundation Scholars Program
scholarships.award_amount: full-tuition leadership scholarship at partner college
scholarships.description: Nomination-based leadership program open to students of all backgrounds in Posse recruitment cities. Scholars receive full-tuition awards at partner colleges after the Dynamic Assessment Process.

verification.notes: Nomination page: not minority- or need-based; open to all backgrounds; must be nominated in a recruitment city; requirements mention academic potential but no national numeric GPA minimum; deadlines vary by city.

application_requirements.label: Be nominated by your high school or a community-based organization
application_requirements.details: You cannot apply to Posse directly. Only a high school or community-based organization registered with Posse, in one of its 20-plus recruitment cities, can nominate you; they may do so as early as the second semester of junior year. The Posse Arts Program and Posse Veterans Program run their nomination process separately.

application_requirements.label: Contact your local Posse office for this year's deadlines
application_requirements.details: Nomination timelines and deadlines vary by Posse city and program; nominations are generally accepted in the spring and summer before senior year begins. Posse asks students to check with the local office at the start of every calendar year.

application_requirements.label: Take part in the Dynamic Assessment Process
application_requirements.details: Posse's selection process, which nominated students participate in. Posse looks for leaders in their high schools and communities, students committed to their education who show academic potential, and students interested in teamwork and diversity.

scholarships.name: Barry Goldwater Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Barry Goldwater Scholarship and Excellence in Education Foundation
scholarships.description: Premier undergraduate award for students pursuing research careers in the natural sciences, engineering, and mathematics. Nominated by participating institutions.

verification.notes: Eligibility page: 3.0 GPA, U.S. citizen/national/territories, STEM sophomores/juniors; institution nomination; research essay; Foundation nomination deadline is the last Friday in January, with internal campus deadlines set locally.

scholarships.name: GE-Reagan Foundation Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Merit-based scholarship for U.S. high school seniors who demonstrate leadership, drive, integrity, and citizenship and plan to enroll full-time at an accredited four-year U.S. college or university. Renewable for up to four years.

verification.notes: Official page: $40,000 ($10,000/yr renewable), 3.0 GPA, U.S. citizen seniors; essay required; 2026 cycle closed Jan 5 2026 — next deadline not yet published.

scholarships.name: Equitable Excellence Scholarship
scholarships.description: Need-based scholarship for high school seniors residing in the 50 U.S. states, D.C., or Puerto Rico who plan to enroll full-time at an accredited two- or four-year U.S. college and show drive to give back to their communities.

verification.notes: Scholarship America page: 2.5 GPA, senior in 50 states/DC/PR, custom essay required; up to $5,000/yr renewable for top 100; application closed — next cycle date not published.

scholarships.name: Hagan Scholarship
scholarships.award_amount: $2,000 plus up to $7,500 per semester (up to 8 semesters)
scholarships.description: Need-based merit scholarship designed to help students graduate college debt-free. Requires enrollment at an eligible four-year nonprofit college, a 3.5 GPA, household income below the program cap, and 240 hours of work before enrolling. Open to applicants regardless of citizenship.

verification.notes: Official application page says all students who meet the eligibility criteria can apply regardless of nationality or citizenship; finalists submit either FAFSA summary if U.S. citizens or proof of non-U.S. citizenship. 2027-2028 fall application opens Sept 1 2026, deadline Dec 1 2026; 3.50 unweighted GPA; no essay.

application_requirements.label: Create an account and submit the application
application_requirements.details: You can apply ONE TIME ONLY, so do not open the application until you are ready. The 2027-2028 application is available from September 1, 2026 for the 2027 graduating class; up to 1,500 scholarships are awarded. Read the sponsor's Scholarship Particulars first: the award must be re-earned every semester. Hagan communicates with applicants only.

application_requirements.label: Provide a current copy of your high school transcript
Provide an "unsealed" letter of recommendation
application_requirements.details: The sponsor specifies the letter must be unsealed.

application_requirements.label: If named a finalist, submit your FAFSA Summary or proof of non-U.S. citizenship
application_requirements.details: Finalists only. U.S. citizens provide the first two pages of their FAFSA Summary, which must show the SAI. Non-U.S. citizens provide proof of non-U.S. citizenship: a copy of a foreign passport or green card.

application_requirements.label: Apply for Federal and State grants if eligible
application_requirements.details: Listed by the sponsor under eligibility criteria with no exceptions.

application_requirements.label: Work 240 hours before your fall disbursement
application_requirements.details: Required to receive the fall semester disbursement, not to apply: 240 hours between January 1 and September 1 of your enrolling year. Must be regularly scheduled non-family employment, performed under supervision, for compensation; full-time or part-time both count.

scholarships.name: Stamps Scholarship (Stamps Scholars Program)
scholarships.sponsor: Stamps Scholars Program / Stamps Family Charitable Foundation
scholarships.description: Merit-based scholarship awarded through 38 partner colleges and universities for students with strong academic merit and leadership; provides multi-year awards plus enrichment funding. Financial need is not considered. Students apply directly to a partner school, and award terms and eligibility vary by institution.

verification.notes: How-to-apply: merit/leadership via 38 partner schools; financial need not considered; apply through partner admission apps — no program-wide GPA, citizenship, or deadline.

scholarships.name: Live Mas Scholarship
scholarships.description: Scholarship for students ages 16-26 who are legal residents of the 50 U.S. states or D.C. (or dependents of active-duty U.S. military) pursuing post-secondary education. Selection is based on passion rather than grades or test scores; applicants submit one response, as either a short video or a 250-500 word essay.

verification.notes: Official Eligibility & FAQs page: 2026 window was Oct 14 2025-Jan 6 2026 at 5:00 PM PST; no GPA, test scores, transcripts, or recommendations required; response may be a 30s-2min video OR a 250-500 word essay; legal-resident/dependent-of-active-duty eligibility does not map cleanly to citizenship tags, so citizenship remains unconfirmed.

application_requirements.label: Apply online during the application window
application_requirements.details: It costs nothing to apply, and there is no GPA or test-score requirement. The 2026 window ran October 14, 2025 to January 6, 2026 at 5:00 PM PST. Mail-in submissions are not accepted. Minors: the instruction email goes to a parent or legal guardian's address.

application_requirements.label: Submit one original response: a video or an essay
application_requirements.details: ONE original response, in either format: a video of 30 seconds to 2 minutes, or an essay of 250-500 words (an essay may include one photo representing your passion). One response addresses all three questions below; the length limit covers the whole response, not each question.

items.prompt: What is your passion and how are you pursuing it? Show your passion and explain what steps you're taking toward it.
What impact do you hope to make with your passion or career path? Share how you're sparking positive change through your educational and career goals.
How do you embody the spirit of Live Más in your everyday life?

scholarships.name: Prudential Emerging Visionaries
scholarships.description: National recognition program for young people ages 14-18 who lead innovative projects addressing financial or societal challenges in their communities. Open to legal residents of the U.S. or Puerto Rico; winners receive up to $15,000.

verification.notes: Changemakers page lists 2023 cycle (closed Nov 2 2023); ages 14-18, up to $15,000; legal residents of U.S. or Puerto Rico — current cycle deadline not published.

scholarships.name: Jackie Robinson Foundation Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Four-year scholarship and comprehensive mentoring for U.S. citizen high school seniors who demonstrate financial need, academic excellence, and leadership, and plan to attend an accredited four-year U.S. college or university.

verification.notes: Apply page: U.S. citizen senior, financial need, four essays; no race/ethnicity requirement listed; 2025-2026 closed Jan 7 2026 — next cycle opens summer 2026.

scholarships.name: National FFA Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: A single application covers many scholarships funded through the National FFA Foundation supporting members pursuing education in agriculture and related fields. Applicants must be FFA members, under age 23, U.S. citizens or permanent residents, and enrolled full-time at a U.S. institution.

verification.notes: Official scholarships page says one application covers multiple opportunities totaling nearly $2.5M for high school senior FFA members or college FFA Alumni. 2025-26 application deadline was Jan 15 2026; no program-wide numeric GPA minimum or next-cycle deadline is published on the page.

scholarships.name: SWE Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Scholarships for students who identify as women pursuing ABET-accredited degrees in engineering, engineering technology, or computing. A single application is considered for all eligible awards. Open only to women.

verification.notes: Official SWE scholarships page says one application considers students for 330+ scholarships and supports undergraduate/graduate degrees in engineering, engineering technology, and related fields. Official FAQ lists level-specific GPA rules: 3.0 unweighted for Collegiate/Graduate applicants, 3.5 unweighted for Emerging First Year applicants, and re-entry/nontraditional students exempt; GPA field is left unknown because the current schema cannot encode applicant-type-specific cutoffs.

scholarships.name: Generation Google Scholarship
scholarships.description: For students pursuing computer science, computer engineering, or a closely related technical field at a U.S. or Canadian university who demonstrate leadership, academic strength, financial need, and commitment to improving representation of underrepresented groups in technology. The U.S. award is $10,000.

verification.notes: Official scholarships landing page returned no published 2026-27 cycle, deadline, or eligibility details on audit date.

scholarships.name: Tylenol Future Care Scholarship
scholarships.description: Merit-based scholarship for college seniors entering graduate school and graduate students enrolled in medical, nursing, or pharmacy programs who will actively treat patients. Open to residents of the 50 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, or D.C.

verification.notes: Official FAQ requires residence in the 50 United States, Puerto Rico, or D.C.; college senior entering graduate school or graduate student status; proof of enrollment in a medical, nursing, or pharmacy graduate program in the 50 United States, Puerto Rico, or D.C.; and one or more years of school remaining. Deadline July 13 2026 11:59pm PT; no citizenship or GPA minimum stated.

scholarships.name: AICPA Foundation Legacy Scholarships
scholarships.description: Scholarships for accounting students pursuing CPA licensure, awarded through a single application. Requires a free AICPA student affiliate membership, full-time enrollment in an accredited accounting/business program, and a minimum 3.0 GPA.

verification.notes: Future CPAs page: 3.0 GPA, U.S. citizen or PR; applications for 2027-2028 funding run Dec 1 2026 through Mar 15 2027.

scholarships.name: James Madison Memorial Fellowship
scholarships.description: Graduate fellowship for aspiring and current secondary-school teachers of American history, American government, or civics who pursue a master's degree focused on the U.S. Constitution. One fellowship per state may be awarded annually; applicants compete within their state of legal residence.

verification.notes: Official eligibility/FAQ pages say applicants must be U.S. citizens planning to teach American history, government, social studies, or political science in grades 7-12; maximum award is $24,000; applications and recommendations are due no later than March 1; no numeric GPA minimum stated.

scholarships.name: YoungArts National Arts Competition
scholarships.description: National arts competition for emerging artists ages 15-18 (grades 10-12) across 10 disciplines including visual, literary, and performing arts. Award winners receive cash prizes from $250 to $10,000 and mentorship; selection is by portfolio or audition.

verification.notes: Apply page: ages 15-18 grades 10-12; citizen, permanent resident, or legally able to receive taxable income in U.S.; portfolio/audition (not essay). 2027 application opens Jul 21 2026; FAQ now publishes a CONFIRMED deadline of Tue Oct 6 2026, 8 PM ET / 5 PM PT, replacing the earlier 2026-10-08 estimate. Application requires an application fee or fee-waiver documentation.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online application before the deadline
application_requirements.details: The 2027 application opens July 21, 2026 and closes Tuesday, October 6, 2026 at 8 PM ET / 5 PM PT. Everything must be uploaded and submitted before the deadline, including payment or a fee waiver. YoungArts strongly recommends not waiting until deadline day.

application_requirements.label: Prepare the portfolio for your discipline and category
application_requirements.details: Depending on discipline, the portfolio is video recordings, audio recordings, documents, or images. There are 10 disciplines: Classical Music, Dance, Design, Film, Jazz, Photography, Theater, Visual Arts, Voice, and Writing. Follow the sponsor's guidelines for the exact discipline and category you enter.

application_requirements.label: Pay the application fee or submit fee-waiver documentation
application_requirements.details: The application is not considered complete until payment is processed or fee-waiver documentation is submitted, and this must happen before the deadline like everything else.

scholarships.name: SMART Scholarship-for-Service Program
scholarships.description: Department of Defense scholarship-for-service covering full tuition, an annual stipend, and summer internships for students in STEM fields. Recipients commit to post-graduation civilian employment with the DoD. Requires U.S. citizenship, a 3.0 GPA, and ability to obtain a security clearance.

verification.notes: Official DoW SMART program PDF: U.S. citizen, 3.0 GPA, STEM degree, security-clearance eligible, service commitment; applications open annually Aug 1 — close date not confirmed on fetched materials.

scholarships.name: UNCF General Scholarship
scholarships.description: Need-based scholarships requiring full-time enrollment at one of UNCF's 37 member historically Black colleges and universities, a minimum 2.5 GPA, and a completed FAFSA. Individual programs have separate eligibility criteria and deadlines.

verification.notes: UNCF scholarships hub: many programs with separate criteria/deadlines; largest private scholarship provider to minority students — no single general deadline published.

scholarships.name: American Indian College Fund Full Circle Scholarship
scholarships.description: Scholarship for enrolled members, or descendants of enrolled members, of state or federally recognized tribes or Alaska Native villages who attend an accredited nonprofit institution full-time with at least a 2.0 GPA. One application is considered for many awards.

verification.notes: College Fund scholarships page: American Indian/Alaska Native students at tribal colleges or nonprofit accredited schools; one annual application opens Feb 1 and students are encouraged to apply by May 31 for priority review; award amounts vary but average $2,000-$3,000.

scholarships.name: Thurgood Marshall College Fund Scholarship
scholarships.description: Scholarships for students attending Thurgood Marshall College Fund member historically Black colleges and universities (HBCUs), predominantly Black institutions (PBIs), and historically Black community colleges. Eligibility, GPA, and requirements vary by individual award.

verification.notes: TMCF scholarships portal: awards for TMCF member-school and eligible accredited students; eligibility varies by scholarship — apply via TMCF Scholarship Portal.

scholarships.name: Harry S. Truman Scholarship
scholarships.description: Merit-based award for institution-nominated college juniors committed to careers in public service. Provides up to $30,000 for graduate study. Open to U.S. citizens and U.S. nationals from American Samoa; permanent residents are not eligible.

verification.notes: Official eligibility page lists citizenship, U.S.-based enrollment, penultimate/final-year status, institution nomination, and graduate-school/public-service intent; no numeric GPA minimum is stated. Application checklist verified 2026-06-24 from Truman Foundation applying pages.

application_requirements.label: Secure institutional nomination
application_requirements.details: Must be nominated by your institution's Truman Faculty Representative after the campus selection process.

application_requirements.label: Complete online application and policy proposal
application_requirements.details: Nominated candidates receive access to an online application including a policy proposal.

application_requirements.label: Three letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Secure three recommendation letters and give them to your campus Advisor for upload into the system.

application_requirements.label: Transcripts from all institutions attended
application_requirements.details: Provide transcripts from any institution attended to your Advisor for upload. All materials due by 11:59 p.m. in your time zone on deadline evening.

application_requirements.label: Advisor submits application to Foundation
application_requirements.details: Advisor reviews the application and sends it to the Foundation when ready. Candidate retains edit access until the deadline.

scholarships.name: Udall Undergraduate Scholarship
scholarships.description: Scholarship for college sophomores and juniors committed to careers related to the environment; Native American and Alaska Native students may also apply in the tribal public policy or Native health care categories. Requires nomination by the student's institution. Award up to $7,500.

verification.notes: Official 2026 eligibility criteria list U.S. citizen/national/PR sophomores or juniors in environmental, Tribal public policy, or Native health-care categories; scholarship page says up to 65 awards of $7,500. 2026 deadline was Mar 4; next deadline not yet published; no numeric GPA minimum stated.

scholarships.name: Fulbright U.S. Student Program
scholarships.sponsor: U.S. Department of State (Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs)
scholarships.award_amount: varies by host country (covers travel, stipend, and living costs)
scholarships.description: Federal program funding graduating college seniors, graduate students, young professionals, and artists to study, conduct research, or teach English abroad in partnership with more than 140 countries. Open to all disciplines; requires U.S. citizenship and a bachelor's degree by the grant start (permanent residents and doctorate holders are not eligible).

verification.notes: Fulbright applicants page: 2027-2028 competition open; national deadline Oct 6 2026 5pm ET; U.S. citizens enrolled in or holding bachelor's by award start.

scholarships.name: NABJ Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Scholarships from the National Association of Black Journalists for student members pursuing journalism or a communications-related field who have at least one year of school remaining. Awards up to $10,000; previous awardees are not eligible to re-apply.

verification.notes: NABJ scholarships page: up to $10,000 for journalism/communications students; previous awardees ineligible — individual program GPA/deadline/membership criteria vary by award.

scholarships.name: ScholarSHPE Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Renewable scholarships for active SHPE members pursuing STEM degrees. Requires SHPE membership and a minimum 2.5 GPA; citizenship is not required for SHPE-funded awards but some sponsor-funded awards do require it.

verification.notes: SHPE page: 2.5 GPA, SHPE membership, STEM major; citizenship not required for SHPE-funded awards; 2026-27 app opens Feb 2 2026 closes Feb 16 2026 — next cycle estimated Feb 2027.

scholarships.name: NSBE Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Scholarships for NSBE members pursuing engineering and STEM degrees. Requires active NSBE membership and a verified GPA in the member profile; individual awards vary by sponsor.

verification.notes: Official NSBE scholarships page lists Spring 2026 awards with a June 30 deadline, required GPA and residency filters, and sponsor-specific GPA/citizenship rules. Examples include 3.0 GPA/U.S. citizen, 3.0 GPA/U.S. citizen or permanent resident, 2.8 GPA/U.S. citizen, 2.5 GPA/U.S. citizen or permanent resident, and 2.0 GPA/U.S. citizen; GPA and citizenship fields are left unknown because there is no single program-wide rule.

scholarships.name: IEEE Presidents' Scholarship
scholarships.description: $10,000 (payable over four years of undergraduate study) awarded to one student finalist at the Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair for an outstanding project in electrical/electronics engineering, computer science, or another IEEE field of interest. Must be an ISEF finalist; no separate application.

verification.notes: Official IEEE Students page says the award is $10,000 payable over four years plus complimentary IEEE Student membership; finalists at Regeneron ISEF are eligible, and IEEE judges review projects in IEEE fields of interest. No separate application, essay, GPA minimum, or citizenship requirement is stated.

scholarships.name: ACM SIGHPC Computational and Data Science Fellowships
scholarships.description: Graduate fellowships for students pursuing a master's or doctoral degree in computational or data science, nominated by the student's graduate research advisor. Targets women and/or members of racial/ethnic groups underrepresented in computing; open to institutions worldwide.

verification.notes: Official SIGHPC fellowship page says nominations for the 2026 cycle ran Mar 15-May 15 2026; awards are $20,000 annually for up to four years for PhD students or two years for master's students. Eligibility is for students pursuing graduate degrees in computational or data science at institutions anywhere in the world; no GPA minimum or citizenship requirement is stated.

application_requirements.label: Have your advisor submit the nomination
application_requirements.details: Submitted by the student's advisor (or soon-to-be advisor), who explains how the candidate qualifies for a fellowship. All three components must be submitted in sequence and completed by the deadline.

application_requirements.label: Submit your CV and candidate statement
application_requirements.details: Submitted by the student, along with contact information for an endorser.

application_requirements.label: Have an endorser submit a brief endorsement
application_requirements.details: Submitted by a current or former instructor, project supervisor, or employer with personal knowledge of your past accomplishments who can speak to your suitability.

scholarships.name: ACS Catalyst Scholarship
scholarships.description: Renewable $10,000 annual scholarship for undergraduates majoring in chemistry or chemistry-related fields (chemistry, biochemistry, chemical engineering, chemical technology) who demonstrate financial need. Not open to pre-med, pharmacy, or nursing students; replaces the former ACS Scholars Program for 2026-2027.

verification.notes: ACS Catalyst page: renewable $10k, 3.0 GPA, U.S. citizen or PR; 2026-27 cycle closed Dec 1 2025–Mar 1 2026; essays and FAFSA required.

application_requirements.label: Create an account and complete the online application
application_requirements.details: Applications ran December 1, 2025 to March 1, 2026 for the 2026-2027 year. All materials, including transcripts, are submitted with the form; you may save and return until the deadline. You must agree to abide by the ACS Code of Conduct.

application_requirements.label: Upload your unofficial transcripts
Prepare a resume or CV
Collect 2 letters of recommendation
Write the personal essays
application_requirements.details: ACS does not publish the essay questions outside the application; its November 2025 information-session recording covers essay strategy.

application_requirements.label: Submit your FAFSA Submission Summary
application_requirements.details: Formerly the Student Aid Report (SAR); required as part of the application.

application_requirements.label: Confirm your submission
application_requirements.details: After submitting you receive a confirmation email from ACS Undergraduate Scholarships (administrator@grantinterface.com). If it does not arrive, check that you clicked Submit, look in spam, and verify your email address. Results are announced in May; if selected, accept by May 30.

scholarships.name: Astronaut Scholarship
scholarships.description: Up to $15,000 for sophomore/junior STEM students intending to pursue research, nominated by faculty at ASF partner universities. Students intending to enter clinical practice are ineligible, though MD/PhD research candidates qualify. U.S. citizenship required.

verification.notes: Official program page says applicants must be U.S. citizens, STEM sophomores/juniors at ASF partner universities, faculty-nominated, research-oriented, and full-time for the supported year. 2026 liaison deadline was Mar 30; next cycle not published; no numeric GPA minimum stated.

scholarships.name: Nurse Corps Scholarship Program
scholarships.award_amount: Full tuition, required fees, and a monthly stipend
scholarships.description: Pays tuition, fees, and a monthly stipend for nursing students (RN and APRN degree programs) in exchange for a service commitment at a Critical Shortage Facility after graduation. Funding preference goes to those with the greatest financial need.

verification.notes: Official 2026 HRSA guidance PDF: tuition/fees/stipend for accredited U.S. RN/APRN programs; citizen/national/PR; 2026 cycle closed Apr 9 2026; personal essays no longer required.

scholarships.name: National Health Service Corps (NHSC) Scholarship Program
scholarships.award_amount: Full tuition, fees, and a monthly stipend
scholarships.description: Pays tuition, fees, and a monthly stipend for students training in primary care health professions (physicians, dentists, nurse practitioners, nurse midwives, physician assistants) in exchange for a minimum two-year service commitment in a Health Professional Shortage Area. Open to U.S. citizens and U.S. nationals only.

verification.notes: Official 2026 NHSC APG PDF: U.S. citizen or national only (no PR); primary care fields; one essay; 2026 cycle closed Apr 16 2026.

scholarships.name: Hertz Fellowship
scholarships.award_amount: Up to $250,000 over five years of PhD study
scholarships.description: Up to five years of PhD funding (valued up to $250,000) for doctoral students in the applied physical and biological sciences, mathematics, or engineering at a U.S. institution. Open to U.S. citizens and permanent residents who are college seniors, first-year graduate students, or in a gap year before graduate school.

verification.notes: Who-can-apply page: citizen or PR; college senior, first-year grad, or gap year; PhD in applied physical/biological sciences, math, or engineering at U.S. institution; 2026 cycle closed — 2027 opens late Aug 2026.

scholarships.name: STEM Stars Actuarial Scholars Program
scholarships.description: $20,000 ($5,000 annually for up to four years) plus mentorship, internships, and networking for high school seniors skilled in math who are interested in the actuarial profession.

verification.notes: Official STEM Stars page says the program offers $20,000 ($5,000 annually for four years), mentoring, internships, and networking for high school students skilled in math and interested in actuarial careers. 2026 season is closed and reopens in fall; no numeric GPA minimum published.

scholarships.name: CAS Trust Scholarship
scholarships.description: Merit-based $2,500 and $5,000 scholarships for full-time college students pursuing a career in property/casualty actuarial science. Requires CAS Student Central membership and having sat for at least one actuarial exam.

verification.notes: Official CAS page says applicants must submit by Jan 31 2026, be full-time college students continuing full-time in 2026-27, have sat for at least one actuarial exam by Mar 31 2026, and be CAS Student Central members. Awards are $5,000 and $2,500; no numeric GPA minimum or citizenship requirement is stated.

scholarships.name: NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program (GRFP)
scholarships.award_amount: $37,000 annual stipend plus $16,000 cost-of-education allowance (3 years)
scholarships.description: Three years of support (a $37,000 annual stipend plus a $16,000 cost-of-education allowance) for students pursuing research-based master's and doctoral STEM degrees. Open to U.S. citizens, nationals, and permanent residents who are senior undergraduates, bachelor's holders, or first-year graduate students.

verification.notes: NSF GRFP: 3-year $37k stipend + $16k COA; citizen/national/PR; STEM research grad study; Nov 2025 field deadlines closed — FY27 cycle dates not yet published.

scholarships.name: AIAA Foundation Undergraduate Scholarships
scholarships.sponsor: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA)
scholarships.description: Scholarships ($500–$10,000) for AIAA student members pursuing science or engineering degrees leading to a career in aerospace. Requires AIAA membership, at least one completed semester, and minimum 3.3 GPA; enrolled at accredited U.S. institution.

verification.notes: AIAA eligibility page: Student member, at least one completed semester, 3.3 GPA for most undergraduate scholarships, aerospace-related engineering/science; applications accepted Oct 15-Jan 31 annually; no single citizenship rule because some awards have award-specific restrictions.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online application by 31 January
application_requirements.details: Applications are accepted 15 October to 31 January and must be submitted by 11:59 PM EST on 31 January. All materials must be in by the deadline for the application to count as complete; AIAA makes no exceptions.

application_requirements.label: Upload your college transcripts as one PDF
application_requirements.details: Transcripts from ALL colleges attended, through the most recent Fall term, combined into a single PDF. Unofficial transcripts are accepted, but official ones are requested if you are offered an award. AIAA does not accept high school transcripts.

application_requirements.label: Write the 500-1,000 word career-objectives essay
application_requirements.details: Specify your career objectives and outline the academic program required to achieve them. Essays must be your own original work; any plagiarized portion disqualifies the application.

application_requirements.label: Answer the award-specific writing prompt
application_requirements.details: Only if applying for a Technical Committee or Section-sponsored award. AIAA does not publish these prompts outside the application.

application_requirements.label: Arrange two professional letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Any combination of academic or career references (advisors, supervisors, bosses, cohort leaders); relatives may not submit them. Submitted through the online system. AIAA's two pages disagree on the letter deadline: the application-requirements page says 13 February, the eligibility page says 14 February. Treat 13 February as the safe date.

application_requirements.label: Prepare a resume or CV
application_requirements.details: Highlight professional experience, extracurricular activities, and awards or certifications.

scholarships.name: American Nuclear Society Scholarships
scholarships.description: Scholarships for ANS student members pursuing nuclear science, nuclear engineering, or a nuclear-related field at accredited U.S. institutions, plus an incoming-freshman award for graduating high school seniors.

verification.notes: ANS page: student member and nuclear-related field; applications open Nov 1, general deadline Feb 1, incoming freshman and community/trade school deadline Apr 1; no single deadline fits all listed grade levels, and no numeric GPA or citizenship rule is stated.

scholarships.name: Tau Beta Pi Scholarship Program
scholarships.sponsor: Tau Beta Pi Association (The Engineering Honor Society)
scholarships.description: Awards of $2,000 (full year) or $1,000 (one semester) supporting the senior year of full-time undergraduate engineering study. Restricted to initiated Tau Beta Pi members.

verification.notes: Official TBP page: members or candidates initiated by June 1 who will be full-time senior undergraduate engineering students in 2026-27; application due Apr 1 and recommendations Apr 8. No citizenship restriction is stated.

scholarships.name: GMiS STEM Scholars Program
scholarships.description: Merit- and service-based scholarships for students pursuing STEM or health-related degrees at accredited U.S. institutions. Minimum 3.0 GPA for merit awards (2.5 for some donor awards); leadership and service required.

verification.notes: GMiS guidelines/FAQ: STEM or health degree, 3.0 GPA, personal statement; Hispanic descent not required; citizen/resident/F1/DACA eligible; May 8 2026 deadline closed.

scholarships.name: AISES A.T. Anderson Memorial Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: American Indian Science and Engineering Society (AISES)
scholarships.description: Merit-based scholarship for AISES members pursuing STEM degrees who are enrolled citizens or descendants of a recognized American Indian Tribe/Alaska Native Village, Native Hawaiian, Pacific Islander, or Indigenous Canadian. Requires AISES membership and a minimum 3.0 GPA.

verification.notes: AISES scholarships page: A.T. Anderson $1k undergrad/$2k grad; AISES member, 3.0 GPA, STEM; tribal/Indigenous eligibility required; applications open Dec 15 and close Apr 30.

scholarships.name: FNSNA Undergraduate Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Foundation of the National Student Nurses' Association (FNSNA)
scholarships.description: Need- and merit-based scholarships (up to $10,000) for students enrolled in state-approved nursing programs (ADN, BSN, diploma, direct-entry master's, RN-to-BSN/MSN, or LPN/LVN-to-RN). High school students are not eligible; open to U.S. citizens and permanent residents.

verification.notes: FNSNA application page: up to $10,000; U.S. citizen or permanent resident; state-approved nursing programs (ADN/BSN/diploma/direct-entry MSN/LPN-RN/accelerated BSN); min 6 credits per semester; high school students ineligible; opens Sep 8 2026, closes Jan 11 2027; no minimum GPA stated.

scholarships.name: AACN2: Pathway to Critical Care Nursing Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: American Association of Critical-Care Nurses & American Association of Colleges of Nursing
scholarships.description: $2,500 scholarships for prelicensure students in entry-level baccalaureate (BSN) nursing programs at AACN member schools who are committed to critical care nursing, have a minimum 3.2 GPA, and will graduate within 6–12 months.

verification.notes: AACN page: eight $2,500 awards; BSN students at AACN member schools, 3.2 GPA, graduating within 6–12 months; applications open Jul 1, close Sep 1 2026.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online application through the AACN scholarship portal
application_requirements.details: Applications open July 1 and close September 1. The portal requires a MyAACN account; AACN does not publish the application's contents outside it.

scholarships.name: BMI Composer Awards (formerly BMI Student Composer Awards)
scholarships.award_amount: awards totaling $20,000 annually, shared among multiple winners
scholarships.description: Annual competition recognizing young composers of classical/concert music who are under age 28. Applicants must reside in the U.S. or its territories, or be citizens of any country in the Western Hemisphere; works are judged anonymously.

verification.notes: BMI page: classical/concert music, under age 28; U.S./territories resident or Western Hemisphere citizen; anonymous score submission; $20k total pool; 2026 deadline was Jan 15 and next-cycle deadline is not yet published.

scholarships.name: The ASCAP Foundation Morton Gould Young Composer Awards
scholarships.description: Honors emerging composers of original concert music who are at least 13 and under age 30. Open to U.S. citizens, permanent residents, or students with a valid U.S. student visa; submissions are scored on a single original work.

verification.notes: 2026 cycle Jan 6–Feb 6 ET; age 13–under 30; U.S. citizen, PR, or valid U.S. student visa; single original concert work; prize amount at Foundation discretion.

scholarships.name: AIGA Worldstudio D×D Scholarships
scholarships.description: Need-based scholarships for students of communication design, illustration, or photography at U.S. colleges who show commitment to social change and diversity. Requires demonstrated financial need; DACA status alone does not qualify.

verification.notes: Rules page: 3.0 GPA, financial need, U.S. citizen or Green Card; communication design, illustration, or photography; 2026/2027 deadline May 6 2026 closed; diversity considered but not required.

scholarships.name: Princess Grace Awards
scholarships.description: Grants supporting extraordinary emerging artists in theater, dance, and film (film accepts directors only). Applicants must live and work in the U.S. (non-citizens need USCIS work authorization) and must be nominated by a nonprofit university, organization, or prior winner; self-nominations are not allowed.

verification.notes: Nomination required; theater, dance, film directors; U.S.-based artists; non-citizens need USCIS work authorization; 2026 Round 1 closed Feb 2.

scholarships.name: ABA Legal Opportunity Scholarship
scholarships.description: Supports incoming first-year law students who demonstrate a strong commitment to advancing diversity, equity, and inclusion and will attend an ABA-accredited law school. Requires a minimum 2.5 undergraduate GPA and a personal statement.

verification.notes: $15,000 over three years to 20–25 first-year law students; 2027 cycle opens Feb 1, closes Apr 15 2027; 2.5 GPA; DEI commitment required, not explicit race restriction.

application_requirements.label: Submit the LOSF application through the Kaleidoscope portal
application_requirements.details: The 2027 application period opens February 1, 2027 and the portal closes at 11:59 p.m. Central Time on April 15, 2027.

application_requirements.label: Write a personal statement
application_requirements.details: Required material. The ABA asks applicants not to submit a resume.

application_requirements.label: Submit an official or unofficial transcript
Collect 2-4 letters of recommendation
Sign the release form inside the LOSF application

scholarships.name: Sigma Tau Delta Scholarships and Awards
scholarships.sponsor: Sigma Tau Delta International English Honor Society
scholarships.description: Annual scholarships for undergraduate and graduate English students who are active members of a Sigma Tau Delta chapter. Requires active society membership plus an application essay and recommendations.

verification.notes: Official scholarship descriptions page lists general requirements of transcript, two recommendations, and scholarship-specific criteria. The William C. Johnson award requires 3.5+ GPA, but most listed scholarships do not publish a numeric GPA floor, so no program-wide minimum is encoded.

scholarships.name: Scholarship for Journalism Diversity Honoring Julie Schoo
scholarships.award_amount: $5,000 per year, renewable up to three years (up to $20,000)
scholarships.description: Scholarship for graduating high school seniors who plan to pursue a journalism career and will bring diversity to U.S. journalism. Requires a 3.0+ GPA and work samples; U.S. citizenship is not required to apply.

verification.notes: Official National Press Club Journalism Institute page was updated May 19 2026 and says to check back in fall 2026 for next-year opportunities. The Julie Schoo scholarship requires high-school senior status, U.S. college application, 3.0 GPA, and journalism-career plans; students do not need to be U.S. citizens.

scholarships.name: RTDNA Foundation Scholarships
scholarships.sponsor: Radio Television Digital News Association (RTDNA) Foundation
scholarships.award_amount: up to $10,000 (varies by scholarship; suite totals $35,000+ annually)
scholarships.description: A suite of national scholarships for college students pursuing careers in radio, television, or digital journalism; recipients must be full-time sophomores, juniors, or seniors. U.S. citizenship is not required, but non-citizens must have a U.S. Taxpayer Identification Number.

verification.notes: Official RTDNA page says scholarships are for full-time sophomores, juniors, or seniors in good standing pursuing radio, television, or digital journalism; key eligibility is studying or working in the United States, with no citizenship restriction stated. 2026 applications open Sep 9 and are due Oct 22; no numeric GPA minimum stated.

scholarships.name: Quill and Scroll Foundation Student Scholarships in Journalism
scholarships.award_amount: up to $1,500 (amounts vary; other awards typically $500)
scholarships.description: Scholarships for high school seniors who intend to major in journalism or a related communications field. Several awards require being a national winner in a Quill and Scroll contest or an inducted senior member.

verification.notes: Official rules page says recipients must major in journalism or a related communications area, submit two recommendations, personal statement, work samples, and a photo. Recommenders report GPA/class rank/test scores if applicable, but no numeric GPA minimum is stated; 2026 application closed May 8.

scholarships.name: PRSSA / PRSA Foundation Scholarships
scholarships.sponsor: Public Relations Student Society of America (PRSSA) / PRSA Foundation
scholarships.description: National scholarships (such as the Betsy Plank/PRSSA Scholarship) for college students pursuing public relations and communications careers. Most awards require active PRSSA membership.

verification.notes: Official PRSSA scholarships and awards page says PRSSA presents more than $100,000 annually to members and chapters; 2025-2026 deadlines vary by award, with many due Apr 30 2026. No program-wide numeric GPA minimum is published.

scholarships.name: DECA Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Merit-based scholarships for current DECA members who intend to study marketing, finance, hospitality, management, or entrepreneurship. Requires active, dues-paying DECA membership.

verification.notes: Official DECA page says 2026-2027 applications are open through Jan 11, 2027; applicants upload a transcript and recommendations, must be current DECA members, and scholarships are merit-based with partner-specific criteria. No program-wide numeric GPA minimum or citizenship restriction is stated.

scholarships.name: FBLA Scholarships
scholarships.award_amount: up to $2,000 (Pearson Futures; other awards vary, e.g. $500 Distinguished Business Leader)
scholarships.description: Scholarships (including the Pearson Futures and Distinguished Business Leader awards) for FBLA members pursuing business-related postsecondary study. Requires active FBLA membership and legal U.S. residency.

verification.notes: Official FBLA x Futures page lists active FBLA membership, legal residence in the U.S., D.C., or territories, high-school junior/senior or undergraduate status, and current/intended enrollment at an accredited U.S. college or university; no numeric GPA minimum is stated.

scholarships.name: National Federation of Music Clubs Competitions and Awards
scholarships.award_amount: more than $750,000 in prizes nationally; amounts vary by competition
scholarships.description: A large slate of national music competitions and scholarships across performance, composition, music education, and music therapy for junior and student/collegiate musicians. Most individual awards require applicants to be native-born or naturalized U.S. citizens (a few endowed awards have exceptions) and, for some categories, NFMC membership.

verification.notes: Official competitions and awards page states NFMC offers more than $750,000 in prizes nationally; most awards require native-born/naturalized U.S. citizen and NFMC membership; deadlines vary per competition on C&A chart.

scholarships.name: Phi Alpha Theta Scholarships and Awards
scholarships.sponsor: Phi Alpha Theta History Honor Society
scholarships.award_amount: multiple undergraduate and graduate student awards; amounts vary by award
scholarships.description: Scholarships, paper prizes, and awards for undergraduate and graduate history students (e.g., the Graydon A. Tunstall Undergraduate Scholarship). Requires membership in the Phi Alpha Theta honor society.

verification.notes: Official prizes and awards page lists 11 student awards and a Mar 1, 2027 next deadline for undergraduate and graduate scholarships. Official membership requirements page lists different GPA rules by level: undergraduates need 3.1 in history and 3.0 overall, while graduate students need better than 3.5; GPA field is left unknown because the current schema cannot encode history-specific and level-specific rules.

scholarships.name: PDK Educational Foundation Scholarship Program
scholarships.sponsor: Phi Delta Kappa International / Educators Rising
scholarships.description: About 100 scholarships annually for students pursuing careers in K-12 public education. Requires membership in Educators Rising, Educators Rising Collegiate, Pi Lambda Theta, or the PDK Association.

verification.notes: Official PDK page says 2026-2027 applications are closed and the 2027-2028 application opens in December. Eligibility requires membership in Educators Rising, Educators Rising Collegiate, Pi Lambda Theta, or PDK; public-education career pursuit; and fall enrollment with tuition/fees. No numeric GPA minimum is stated.

scholarships.name: Princeton Prize in Race Relations
scholarships.sponsor: Princeton University (Princeton Prize in Race Relations)
scholarships.description: Cash award recognizing high school students (grades 9-12) who, through volunteer work, have demonstrated leadership in advancing racial equity or understanding in their school or community. Open to all students regardless of identity; not restricted by field of study.

verification.notes: Official apply page: $2,500; currently enrolled grades 9-12 in the United States; volunteer racial-equity leadership; open regardless of identity, with no citizenship gate stated; 2026 cycle closed Jan 15.

scholarships.name: Psi Chi Undergraduate Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Psi Chi, The International Honor Society in Psychology
scholarships.description: Scholarship for undergraduate psychology students who are members of Psi Chi. Requires active Psi Chi membership; the society also offers numerous research and travel grants.

verification.notes: Eight $3,000 scholarships; active Psi Chi undergraduate member; financial need and academic excellence; due June 29 annually.

scholarships.name: Hispanic Heritage Youth Awards

scholarships.name: LULAC National Scholarship Fund

scholarships.name: MALDEF Law School Scholarship Program

scholarships.name: Cobell Scholarship
scholarships.award_amount: merit-based award, disbursed according to need and institution financial-aid standards
scholarships.description: For enrolled members of U.S. federally recognized Tribes or Alaska Native corporations pursuing vocational, undergraduate, graduate, or professional degrees at accredited nonprofit institutions.

scholarships.name: Native Forward Scholars Fund

scholarships.name: Paul & Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans
scholarships.sponsor: Paul & Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans

scholarships.name: TheDream.US National Scholarship

scholarships.description: For first-generation immigrant students who are undocumented (with or without DACA or TPS), came to the U.S. before age 16 and before Nov. 1, 2020, and attend a partner college; funds tuition and fees at one of TheDream.US Partner Colleges.

verification.notes: Official page says next round opens Nov 1 2026; 2026-27 round closed Feb 28 2026. Eligibility includes undocumented first-generation immigrant students with or without DACA/TPS, 2.5 GPA, significant unmet need, and Partner College enrollment.

scholarships.name: Tillman Scholars Program

verification.notes: Official page says the average award is $10,000 per academic year and varies by financial need and educational expenses; deadline varies yearly and the application window can range from Nov-Feb.

scholarships.name: AMVETS National Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: For U.S. citizen veterans and active-duty/Guard/Reserve service members, as well as the spouses, children, and grandchildren of veterans or active-duty members, pursuing an undergraduate degree; demonstrated financial need required.

Kaleidoscope child/grandchild application lists U.S. citizenship, veteran relationship, high-school senior status, financial need, transcript/GPA status, FAFSA summary, and essay; no numeric GPA minimum is stated.

scholarships.name: Scholarships for Military Children
scholarships.description: For unmarried military dependent children under age 23 with a valid Uniformed Services ID card whose sponsor is active duty, reserve/guard, retired, or deceased; for full-time undergraduate study (high school applicants 3.0 GPA, college applicants 2.5 GPA).

scholarships.name: Folds of Honor Higher Education Scholarship
scholarships.award_amount: unmet-need award; maximum payment $2,500 per term for full-time students

verification.notes: Official page lists an annual Feb 1-Mar 31 application window, unmet-need basis, 2.0 term GPA requirement after award, and $2,500 maximum payment per term for full-time higher-education students.

scholarships.name: Jesse Brown Memorial Youth Scholarship
scholarships.description: For youth volunteers age 21 or younger who have completed at least 100 lifetime volunteer hours serving veterans through DAV or the DAV Auxiliary; usable at any accredited institution of higher learning.

verification.notes: Official page lists $140k awarded annually with awards from $2,500-$30,000; eligibility is age 21 or younger plus at least 100 volunteer hours through DAV or DAV Auxiliary; no numeric GPA minimum stated; 2026 deadline was Feb 28.

scholarships.name: Point Foundation Flagship Scholarship

scholarships.description: For LGBTQ+ and ally students (at least a high school senior) enrolled full-time in a bachelor's, graduate, or professional degree at an accredited, not-for-profit U.S. college or university; requires a connection and commitment to the LGBTQ+ community.

verification.notes: Official Flagship page lists a need-based financial award renewable for up to four years; 3.3 GPA; opens Sept 10 and closes Nov 20, 2026 at 8pm ET for fall 2026 funding.

scholarships.name: JACL National Scholarship Program
scholarships.award_amount: 30+ one-time awards totaling more than $70,000 annually

verification.notes: Official page says the program offers over 30 one-time awards with more than $70,000 total annually; JACL membership required and open to all ethnic backgrounds; 2026 freshman deadline was Mar 2 and other categories Apr 3.

scholarships.name: OCA-UPS Gold Mountain Scholarship
scholarships.description: Administered by OCA - Asian Pacific American Advocates for first-generation college students (whose parents did not complete a college degree); applicants need not be U.S. citizens, but DACA/undocumented students must have an SSN or ITIN.

verification.notes: Official OCA scholarships page lists the UPS Gold Mountain Scholarship as $2,000 for first-generation Asian American, Native Hawaiian, and Pacific Islander high-school seniors. scholarships.name: Lime Connect Pathways Scholarship for High School Seniors
scholarships.description: For current high school seniors with a visible or invisible disability who are pursuing a degree at a four-year university or college in the U.S. or Canada; requires joining the (free) Lime Network.

No GPA minimum or citizenship requirement is stated.

scholarships.name: Microsoft Disability Scholarship

scholarships.name: Anne Ford Scholarship
scholarships.description: For graduating high school seniors with a documented learning disability (LD) who will enroll full-time in a four-year bachelor's degree program; demonstrated financial need required.

scholarships.name: Pro Deo and Pro Patria Scholarships
scholarships.award_amount: up to $1,500 per year (renewable up to four years)
scholarships.description: For a Knights of Columbus member in good standing (or the son/daughter of such a member or deceased member, or a Columbian Squire) entering freshman year full-time in a bachelor's program at a Catholic college or university in the United States.

scholarships.name: Coolidge Scholarship
scholarships.award_amount: full ride (tuition, room, board, and expenses for four years of undergraduate study)
scholarships.description: Full-ride, need-blind merit scholarship for U.S. high school juniors to attend any accredited U.S. college or university; emphasizes academic excellence and interest in public policy. Application includes multiple essays, and current high school seniors are not eligible to apply.

verification.notes: Official timeline says 2026-27 applications will open in late summer or early fall 2026 and the submission deadline is TBD. Full-ride for HS juniors (fall 2028 enrollment); citizen or LPR. Checklist verified 2026-06-24 from Coolidge FAQ.

application_requirements.label: Complete online application forms
application_requirements.details: Complete Profile, Honesty Statement, Academic Information, and other required portal forms. Submit only when all forms show Complete.

application_requirements.label: Three application essays
application_requirements.details: Application includes three essays. Essays 2 and 3 require reading The Autobiography of Calvin Coolidge.

items.prompt: What are the next intellectual frontiers you hope to explore in your college years? How do you envision you might use the knowledge you gain?

Write an essay explaining and evaluating the rationale behind Coolidge's response to the 1927 floods. How did Coolidge imagine the problems presented by the floods would be solved absent a strong federal government response?

Describe and evaluate the tax reforms under Harding and Coolidge, which they called 'Scientific Taxation.' What was the logic behind the 1920s tax reforms? What does evidence from the Harding and Coolidge years tell us about the impact of the 1920s tax reforms?

application_requirements.label: Two letters of reference
application_requirements.details: Exactly two letters required, not from family or peers. Recommenders upload via a separate portal; you may submit before letters arrive.

application_requirements.label: High school transcript and academic info
application_requirements.details: Unofficial transcript acceptable at application stage. Report cumulative GPA and maximum possible cumulative GPA from the most recent transcript available.

scholarships.name: Jack Kent Cooke Foundation Young Scholars Program

scholarships.description: Selective five-year pre-college scholarship for high-achieving 7th-grade students with financial need (family adjusted gross income up to $95,000); provides educational advising and funding for enrichment through high school. Requires mostly A's since 6th grade.

verification.notes: Official Cooke Young Scholars page: 7th grade/rising 8th grade, mostly As, financial need, currently attending middle school in the U.S. and planning to attend high school in the U.S.; no citizenship restriction is stated. 2026 application window was Feb 5-Apr 29.
