verification.notes: Ages 13-19 civic project + essay; citizen or U.S.-based; $10k grand prize — 2026 deadline May 3 2026 11:59pm PT closed.

scholarships.name: DoSomething.org Scholarships
scholarships.description: Scholarship opportunities for young people ages 13-25 who complete volunteer or community-service campaigns; no GPA, essay, or recommendations required, and winners are selected by random drawing. Open to U.S. residents and U.S. citizens living abroad.

scholarships.name: Rhodes Scholarship

application_requirements.label: Apply for Oxford postgraduate study
application_requirements.details: Rhodes Scholarship supports full-time postgraduate study at the University of Oxford.

scholarships.name: Marshall Scholarship

scholarships.description: Funds up to 50 U.S. citizens per year for graduate study in any field at any UK university. Requires a first undergraduate degree (minimum 3.7 GPA) earned within roughly two years of applying; applicants who already hold a UK degree are ineligible.

verification.notes: Up to 50 U.S. citizens/yr for UK graduate study; 3.7 GPA; institutional endorsement; 2026 cycle closed Sep 16 2025 — 2027 opens late spring 2026.

scholarships.name: Gates Cambridge Scholarship

application_requirements.label: Submit Cambridge graduate admission and funding application

items.prompt: Please describe how, through your education and past experiences, you have displayed academic excellence.
Please explain why you want to study this specific course at the University of Cambridge, and how it will facilitate your future plans.

Please provide an example, or examples, of how you have demonstrated a commitment to improving the lives of others. Please explain why this is important to you and how it is relevant to your future plans.

Please provide an example, or examples, of how you have demonstrated a capacity for leadership. This could be by initiating a change or bringing others together to achieve a goal. How will this help you make an impact in the future?

application_requirements.label: Gates Cambridge reference

scholarships.award_amount: Georgia residents: tuition, fees, and books (~$12,500/year); out-of-state: non-resident tuition waiver (~$21,000/year)
scholarships.description: Four-year merit scholarship and scholar community for top incoming Georgia Tech freshmen (~10-20 per year), selected on scholarship, leadership, progress, and service. No separate application; apply for first-year admission by Early Action (Georgia residents: October 15; non-Georgia students: November 2 for fall 2027 entry).

application_requirements.label: Submit a Georgia Tech Early Action application
application_requirements.details: Apply by October 15 if you are a Georgia resident or November 2 if you are not. There is no separate Gold Scholars application and no additional materials are accepted; selection is made from your admission application.

application_requirements.label: Write a strong essay and fully document your activities in that application
application_requirements.details: Because no extra materials are accepted, the personal essay and activities sections of your Georgia Tech admission application are the basis for Gold Scholars selection.

items.prompt: Why do you want to study your chosen major, and why do you want to study that major at Georgia Tech?

verification.notes: Gold Scholars benefits page plus GT admission deadlines: scholarship consideration requires Early Action admission application.

scholarships.name: Academic Excellence Scholarship (AES)
scholarships.sponsor: The University of Texas at Dallas
scholarships.award_amount: varies by cohort (recipients qualify for in-state tuition and fees)

eligible_schools.name: The University of Texas at Dallas

scholarships.description: Merit scholarship for first-time-in-college freshmen admitted to UT Dallas for the fall term immediately after high school graduation. The admission application serves as the AES application; holistic review may consider optional essay, resume, and recommendations. AES recipients qualify for in-state tuition regardless of residency.

application_requirements.label: Submit a UT Dallas first-year application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the Common App or ApplyTexas by December 1. The admission application is the AES application; there is no separate AES form.

application_requirements.label: Submit official high school transcripts
application_requirements.details: Official high school transcripts are required as part of the application materials reviewed for AES.

application_requirements.label: Add the encouraged supplemental materials
application_requirements.details: Encouraged but not required: UT Dallas invites letters of recommendation (maximum of 3), a resume, and/or an essay with your application for its holistic review. The sponsor sets no essay prompt or topic.

application_requirements.label: Complete the FAFSA (encouraged)
application_requirements.details: UT Dallas encourages completing the FAFSA, though it is not required for AES consideration.

application_requirements.label: If offered an AES award, accept it by June 1
application_requirements.details: The deadline to accept an AES award offer is June 1.

verification.notes: UT Dallas AES prospective page: Dec 1 deadline, fall freshman only, no separate AES application.

scholarships.name: CMU Pathway Program
scholarships.award_amount: tuition-free for families under $75k/year; meets 100% demonstrated need for eligible domestic undergraduates
scholarships.description: Need-based institutional aid for admitted Carnegie Mellon undergraduates. CMU does not offer general merit scholarships; admitted domestic students may receive grants through the Pathway Program (including tuition-free attendance for families earning under $75,000) after submitting the FAFSA and CSS Profile. Aid deadlines follow CMU financial aid timelines, not a single merit-scholarship date.

application_requirements.label: Submit the FAFSA for CMU financial aid
application_requirements.details: CMU requires both the FAFSA and CSS Profile for need-based aid. Forms open October 1; CMU's preferred deadlines are about November 15 (Early Decision) and February 15 (Regular Decision).

application_requirements.label: Submit the CSS Profile for CMU financial aid
application_requirements.details: Required alongside the FAFSA. Same preferred deadlines: about November 15 (Early Decision) and February 15 (Regular Decision).

application_requirements.label: Provide signed tax documents if CMU requests them
application_requirements.details: CMU may request signed parent and student tax returns, W-2s, or other supporting documents to verify your aid application.

verification.notes: CMU SFS FAQ confirms no general merit scholarships; costs-aid page confirms Pathway thresholds and full-need commitment.

scholarships.name: NOAA Ernest F. Hollings Undergraduate Scholarship
scholarships.award_amount: up to $9,500 per academic year for two years plus paid NOAA summer internship
scholarships.description: Two-year undergraduate scholarship for sophomores at accredited U.S. colleges and universities, with a paid NOAA internship between the first and second award years. Supports students pursuing NOAA-mission fields including oceanic, atmospheric, and environmental sciences.

application_requirements.label: Complete the Hollings online application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the Hollings Scholarship website during the annual September–January window.

application_requirements.label: Answer Hollings essay questions
application_requirements.details: Essay responses are required components of the online application.

application_requirements.label: Upload official college transcript(s)
application_requirements.details: One official transcript for each institution attended must be uploaded into the online application.

application_requirements.label: Submit two academic references
application_requirements.details: Two references must be received by the published deadline; incomplete applications are ineligible.

verification.notes: NOAA FAQ and news: up to $9,500/year for two years plus paid internship; sophomore status; 3.0 GPA; class of 2027 opens Sept 1, 2026 and closes Jan 31, 2027.

scholarships.name: Boren Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Defense Language and National Security Education Office (via Boren Awards)
scholarships.award_amount: up to $25,000 (duration-based; maximum for 25–52 weeks abroad)
scholarships.description: Funds study abroad in world regions critical to U.S. interests for matriculated U.S. undergraduate students who propose language study outside Western Europe, Canada, Australia, or New Zealand. Recipients commit to federal national-security service after graduation.

application_requirements.label: Start a Boren Scholarship application online
application_requirements.details: Begin at the Boren Awards Apply Now page and complete eligibility determination before other sections unlock.

application_requirements.label: Contact your Boren campus representative early
application_requirements.details: Campus representatives may set earlier on-campus deadlines for feedback before the national deadline.

application_requirements.label: Identify or design an eligible overseas study program
application_requirements.details: Proposed study must be in an eligible destination and language outside Western Europe, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand.

application_requirements.label: Complete essays and required application sections
application_requirements.details: Boren guidance recommends drafting essays early and sharing them with recommenders before the national deadline.

items.prompt: Please discuss the following points in one integrated essay, giving equal attention to each point. Explain the significance of your overseas destination, region, and language to U.S. national security. You should make a specific, detailed, and focused argument relevant to your academic interests, as well as your professional goals for national security employment within the U.S. government. Priority organizations (Tier One) are the Departments of War, State, and Homeland Security, as well as the intelligence Community.

Please discuss the following points in one integrated essay, giving equal attention to each point. You can discuss the points in any order. Think about a previous experience that has led to growth or a personal quality. Reflect upon it and describe how it will assist you in preparing for and spending significant time overseas studying a critical foreign language and culture and, if applicable, conducting your proposed research. Explain how the destination and language you selected will help you achieve your career goals, including your plans to fulfill the federal service requirement. Be specific. If appropriate, you may also include past academic, extracurricular, volunteer, internship, and professional experiences relevant to your federal career goals. As you will be committing to working for the federal government for at least one year, describe what makes you interested in federal service and what you will bring as a leader in the federal workforce.

verification.notes: Official homepage: 2027 Boren Scholarship national deadline Jan 27, 2027; max awards by duration; U.S. citizens only; matriculated undergraduates.

scholarships.name: National Eagle Scout Association (NESA) Scholarships
scholarships.award_amount: varies by award ($6,000–$50,000; multiple CST and national awards)
scholarships.description: Merit scholarships for Eagle Scouts who are NESA members and full-time students in degree or skilled-trade programs. Awards recognize Scouting participation, school and community service, and character; one-time awards with multiple national and council-service-territory tiers.

application_requirements.label: Confirm NESA membership
application_requirements.details: You must be a NESA member to apply; membership is verified through the application system.

application_requirements.label: Complete the NESA scholarship application in the portal
application_requirements.details: Create or use your scholarship database login; applications are submitted online when the portal is open.

application_requirements.label: Confirm Eagle Board of Review date meets cutoff
application_requirements.details: For the 2025–2026 cycle, Eagle Board of Review must be on or before January 24, 2026; check the official page for the next cycle cutoff.

verification.notes: Official NESA page: NESA member/Eagle Scout; senior year through college junior/associate halfway or skilled-trade student; full-time accredited program; not military academy/graduate student. Portal opens Dec 1, 2026. No GPA or citizenship gate is stated.

scholarships.name: Scholarship America Dream Award
scholarships.award_amount: up to $10,000 per year (renewable up to three additional years)
scholarships.description: Renewable scholarship for current college undergraduates who have completed at least one year of full-time study and are pursuing a first associate's or bachelor's degree. High school seniors are not eligible. Financial need is emphasized in selection.

application_requirements.label: Apply through Scholarship America when the Dream Award opens
application_requirements.details: Use the official Dream Award page; sign up for Remind Me notifications when the program is closed.

application_requirements.label: Submit semifinalist documents if invited
application_requirements.details: Semifinalists must provide an official transcript and financial documentation such as FAFSA Submission Summary.

verification.notes: Official page: 3.0 GPA; U.S. citizen, PR, or DACA; current undergrads only (not HS seniors); up to $10,000 renewable; applications closed for current cycle; reopening fall 2026 per Scholarship America student page.

scholarships.name: Sallie Mae Bridging the Dream Scholarship for High School Seniors
scholarships.sponsor: The Sallie Mae Fund (administered by Thurgood Marshall Scholarship Fund)
scholarships.description: Needs-based scholarship contest for Pell-eligible high school seniors planning full-time undergraduate study within one year of graduation. Up to thirty awards of up to $10,000 each.

application_requirements.label: Submit entry through the TMCF open scholarships portal
application_requirements.details: Apply at tmcf.org/students-alumni/scholarships/open-scholarships/ during the published contest period.

application_requirements.label: Upload transcript for most recently completed term
application_requirements.details: Official or unofficial transcript must include end-of-term grades and cumulative GPA.

application_requirements.label: Upload FAFSA Student Aid Report
application_requirements.details: 2026–2027 official rules require a copy of the AY 26–27 FAFSA Student Aid Report.

application_requirements.label: Submit required video response
application_requirements.details: Video must address the community-values prompt in the official rules.

items.prompt: What core principles or values do you believe are most essential to creating positive change in your community and future career field? How do you plan to embody them in both your educational pursuits and interactions with others?

verification.notes: Official 2026–2027 PDF: March 16–April 24, 2026 contest period; Pell-eligible; 2.75 GPA; up to 30 awards of up to $10,000; next-cycle dates not yet published.

scholarships.name: Sallie Mae Bridging the Dream Scholarship for Graduate Students
scholarships.sponsor: The Sallie Mae Fund (administered by Thurgood Marshall College Fund)
scholarships.description: Graduate-student scholarship contest administered with Thurgood Marshall College Fund for legal residents enrolled in accredited U.S. graduate programs. Official landing page publishes the active application window for the current award year.

application_requirements.label: Apply through the TMCF open scholarships portal
application_requirements.details: Follow the apply link from the official Sallie Mae Bridging the Dream for Graduate Students landing page.

application_requirements.label: Submit required video entry
application_requirements.details: See the official rules PDF linked from the landing page for the current video prompt and entry requirements.

verification.notes: Official landing page and rules PDF: AY 2026–2027 applications open June 1 through July 17, 2026; up to 20 scholarships of up to $10,000 each; 3.0 GPA and graduate enrollment required.

scholarships.name: Phi Kappa Phi Fellowship
scholarships.sponsor: The Honor Society of Phi Kappa Phi
scholarships.description: National graduate-study fellowships for active Phi Kappa Phi members entering their first year of full-time graduate or professional study. Each chapter nominates one candidate for the society-wide competition.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online fellowship application and save the PDF
application_requirements.details: Apply through the Phi Kappa Phi awards portal; save the completed PDF for chapter submission.

application_requirements.label: Submit to your local chapter by its deadline
application_requirements.details: Submit application PDF, official transcripts, 2,500-word work sample, and two recommendation letters to your chapter.

application_requirements.label: Confirm active Phi Kappa Phi membership
application_requirements.details: Must be active (dues paid) through April 15 of the award year or accepted for initiation by June 30.

verification.notes: Official Fellowship page: 40 awards at $8,500, 6 at $15,000, 2 at $35,000; active/initiated member nominated by chapter; first full-time graduate/professional study; no more than 9 graduate credits; national deadline Apr 15, 2026; no GPA floor stated.

scholarships.name: Samsung American Legion Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Samsung (endowment administered by The American Legion)
scholarships.award_amount: up to $10,000 (national scholars); $5,000 runners-up; $1,250 department finalists
scholarships.description: Scholarships for high school juniors who attend the current American Legion Boys State or ALA Girls State session and are direct descendants of eligible wartime veterans. Awards are tiered at national and department-finalist levels.

application_requirements.label: Complete the Samsung American Legion online application
application_requirements.details: Create a user account on the application page and submit during the Boys State/Girls State session window.

application_requirements.label: Attend current Boys State or ALA Girls State
application_requirements.details: Applicants must participate in the current session of Legion Boys State or ALA Girls State.

verification.notes: Official Legion FAQ: $10k national scholars, $5k runners-up, $1,250 department finalists; due no later than first day of Boys/Girls State per ALA FAQ.

scholarships.name: American Legion Eagle Scout of the Year Scholarship
scholarships.description: National scholarship for Eagle Scouts nominated through their American Legion department. One national winner receives $10,000; three runners-up receive $2,500 each. Nomination window runs November 1 through March 1 annually.

application_requirements.label: Submit Eagle Scout of the Year nomination packet to your department
application_requirements.details: Packets must be received by the applicant's American Legion department no later than March 1.

application_requirements.label: Use the official nomination form
application_requirements.details: Download the Eagle Scout of the Year Nomination Form from the official scholarship page during the nomination window.

verification.notes: Official nomination form: $10,000 winner and three $2,500 runners-up; Legion-family/BSA affiliation, Eagle Scout, religious emblem, practical citizenship, age 15+ and high-school enrollment; department nominations due Mar 1; no GPA or citizenship gate stated.

scholarships.name: ALA Children of Warriors National Presidents' Scholarship
scholarships.description: Fifteen $5,000 scholarships for high school seniors who are direct descendants of veterans who served during American Legion membership eligibility dates and were honorably discharged. Legion Family membership is not required.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online application at ALAforVeterans.org
application_requirements.details: Application is at www.ALAforVeterans.org/Scholarships/Children-of-Warriors; submitted online to National Headquarters.

application_requirements.label: Document community service hours if applicable
application_requirements.details: Volunteer hours for ALA/Legion Family or other organizations may require signed documentation per FAQ.

verification.notes: Official ALA FAQ: 15×$5,000; senior only; veteran descendant; deadline March 1 at 11:59 p.m. Eastern annually.

scholarships.name: P.E.O. STAR Scholarship
scholarships.description: Non-renewable $2,500 scholarship for graduating women recommended by a local P.E.O. chapter. Open to U.S. or Canadian citizens or permanent residents planning accredited postsecondary study.

application_requirements.label: Secure a local P.E.O. chapter recommendation
application_requirements.details: Students must be recommended by a local chapter; chapters submit recommendations online August 15–October 15.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online STAR application within 30 days
application_requirements.details: After chapter recommendation, the Executive Office emails a link; submit profile, 700-word essay, two recommendations, and transcript within 30 days.

verification.notes: Official P.E.O. page: $2,500 non-renewable; 3.0 GPA; women; chapter recommendation required; chapter window Aug 15–Oct 15.

scholarships.name: NAACP Inspire Initiatives Scholarships
scholarships.description: Portfolio of need-based and merit scholarships administered through the NAACP Inspire Initiatives portal for members enrolled or accepted at accredited U.S. colleges. Individual awards have additional field, demographic, or activity requirements.

application_requirements.label: Join or confirm NAACP membership
application_requirements.details: Applicants must be NAACP members for Inspire Initiatives scholarships.

application_requirements.label: Apply through the Inspire Initiatives scholarship portal
application_requirements.details: Gather transcript, FAFSA Submission Summary, resume, headshot, and activity list; apply when the portal is open.

verification.notes: Official NAACP scholarships page: 3.0 GPA; membership required; 2026 portal April 13–May 22; next-cycle dates not yet published.

scholarships.name: Agnes Jones Jackson Scholarship
scholarships.description: Non-renewable $2,000 NAACP Inspire Initiatives scholarship for members under age 25 who demonstrate financial need and are enrolled or accepted at accredited U.S. institutions.

application_requirements.label: Apply through the NAACP Inspire Initiatives portal
application_requirements.details: Apply when the portal is open; under-25 and financial-need requirements apply.

verification.notes: Official NAACP page: $2,000; NAACP membership; 3.0 GPA; under 25; financial need; 2026 cycle April 13–May 22.

scholarships.name: NIH Undergraduate Scholarship Program (UGSP)
scholarships.award_amount: tuition and educational expenses plus paid NIH research training (see official UGSP terms)
scholarships.description: Scholarship and research-training program for disadvantaged-background undergraduates committed to biomedical, behavioral, or social science research careers. Includes paid summer research at NIH and a post-graduation service commitment.

application_requirements.label: Create NIH Application Center profile
application_requirements.details: Complete profile and start the Undergraduate Scholarship Program application in the NIH Application Center.

application_requirements.label: Pass the UGSP eligibility screener
application_requirements.details: Confirm you meet citizenship, enrollment, GPA, and exceptional-financial-need requirements before submitting.

application_requirements.label: Submit personal statement and coursework list
application_requirements.details: Application requires coursework history and a personal statement describing research interests and goals.

application_requirements.label: Arrange Exceptional Financial Need certification
application_requirements.details: Financial aid office must complete EFN certification by the published deadline.

application_requirements.label: Submit three references
application_requirements.details: References must be received by the published reference deadline.

verification.notes: Official UGSP page: 3.3 GPA or top 5%; U.S. citizen or PR; full-time undergrad; exceptional financial need; 2026 cycle closed March 31, 2026; service payback required.

scholarships.name: The American Legion Legacy Scholarship
scholarships.award_amount: up to $20,000 (children of the fallen) or up to $5,000 (children of disabled veterans)
scholarships.description: Needs-based scholarship for children of post-9/11 servicemembers who died on active duty or post-9/11 veterans with a VA disability rating of 50% or higher. Covers qualified educational expenses after other aid; recipients may reapply up to six times.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online Legacy Scholarship application
application_requirements.details: Apply through register.legion.org/legacy-scholarship-application; create a Legion.org account to save progress.

application_requirements.label: Upload required military and disability documentation
application_requirements.details: Requires DD1300, DD214/NGB Form 22, or official VA disability rating paperwork as applicable.

application_requirements.label: Submit financial information for need-based review
application_requirements.details: Legacy Scholarship is needs-based; portal determines eligibility after financial data entry.

verification.notes: Official Legion news and application page: post-9/11 fallen or 50%+ disabled veteran parent; up to $20k/$5k; opens annually in January; 2026 application open; closing date not published on FAQ.

scholarships.name: Elks Emergency Educational Grant
scholarships.award_amount: up to $5,000 per year (renewable up to four years)
scholarships.description: Renewable one-year grants up to $5,000 for unmarried undergraduate students under 24 whose Elk parent died or is totally disabled. Demonstrated financial need required; applications available by request starting May 1.

application_requirements.label: Request an EEG application from ENF
application_requirements.details: Email scholarship@elks.org or call 773-755-4732 starting May 1, 2026 to request the application.

application_requirements.label: Return completed application to ENF by December 31
application_requirements.details: All applications must be received by the ENF Scholarship Office by December 31, 2026.

verification.notes: Official ENF page: up to $5,000; child/stepchild of deceased or totally disabled Elk; financial need; full-time accredited U.S. undergraduate; unmarried and under 24; applications available May 1, 2026; no GPA or citizenship gate stated.

scholarships.name: ALA Non-Traditional Student Scholarship
scholarships.description: Five $2,000 scholarships (one per division) for returning students who are Legion Family members in good standing, honorably discharged veterans, or spouses of active-duty servicemembers or veterans. Part-time and graduate study eligible.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online application at ALAforVeterans.org
application_requirements.details: Application at https://www.ALAforVeterans.org/Scholarships/Non-Traditional-Student-Scholarship submitted to National Headquarters.

verification.notes: Official ALA FAQ: $2,000×5; Legion Family/veteran/spouse paths; deadline March 1 at 11:59 p.m. Eastern annually.

scholarships.name: NHS Scholarship

scholarships.award_amount: part of $2,000,000 awarded to 600 seniors annually (individual award varies)
scholarships.description: National scholarship for high school seniors who are active NHS members in good standing, verified by their chapter adviser, and planning to pursue a degree at an accredited U.S. college, university, military institute, or trade school. Recipients are selected on NHS pillars: Scholarship, Service, Leadership, and Character.

application_requirements.label: Create or use an NHS student account verified by your adviser
application_requirements.details: Applicants must be NHS student account holders verified by the chapter adviser before applying.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online NHS Scholarship application when open
application_requirements.details: Application opens September 3, 2026 and closes November 20, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. ET.

verification.notes: Official NHS Scholarship page: high school senior, active NHS member in good standing, adviser-verified NHS account, and plan to pursue a degree at an accredited U.S. college, military institute, or trade school. Application opens Sept 3, 2026 and deadline is Nov 20, 2026; no citizenship restriction is stated.

scholarships.name: AFCEA STEM Majors Scholarship

application_requirements.label: Complete the AFCEA STEM Majors online application

application_requirements.details: Recommender submits a PDF letter to edfoundation@afcea.org; applicant coordinates submission.

scholarships.name: Jeannette Rankin Foundation Scholar Grant
scholarships.award_amount: up to $2,500 annually, renewable up to five years
scholarships.description: Need-based grants for women and nonbinary applicants age 35 and older working toward a first associate's or bachelor's degree. The Foundation supports students from all states and backgrounds who demonstrate financial need.

application_requirements.label: Apply during the Scholar Grant application period
application_requirements.details: Application period opens November 2026 per the official homepage; apply through rankinfoundation.org when open.

verification.notes: Official National Scholar Grant page: women/nonbinary age 35+; technical/vocational, associate's, or first bachelor's degree; financial need; up to $2,500 annually, renewable up to five years; 2025-2026 cycle closed Feb 13 2026 and next application opens Nov 2026.

scholarships.name: Mensa Foundation Scholarship

scholarships.description: Essay-based scholarships awarded from a single online application. Applicants write a 550-word or fewer essay outlining academic and professional goals and how personal experiences support them.

application_requirements.label: Complete the Mensa Foundation AwardSpring application
application_requirements.details: One portal application considers you for all Mensa Foundation scholarships for which you qualify.

application_requirements.label: Submit the 550-word goal essay in plain text
application_requirements.details: Essay must be original, in English, and must not include identifying information in the essay body.

items.prompt: Clearly detail your goals and how your experiences support them.

verification.notes: Official Mensa Foundation page: scholarships support students worldwide, use a 550-word goals essay, require proof of enrollment if awarded, and do not state a GPA or citizenship restriction. The official page still lists the 2025-26 cycle closing Jan 15, 2026; the next-cycle deadline is left unknown until posted.

scholarships.name: Courageous Persuaders Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Detroit Auto Dealers Association (DADA) Educational Foundation
scholarships.description: National video scholarship competition where high school students create 30-second television commercials promoting safe driving. Middle school classrooms judge entries in categories such as impaired driving, distracted driving, and passenger empowerment.

application_requirements.label: Submit a 30-second safe-driving commercial
application_requirements.details: Enter through the official video contest form; entry deadline March 30, 2026.

verification.notes: Official rules: 2025/2026 competition open to high school students in the continental United States, grades 9-12 and age 19 or under for teams, with valid government-issued photo ID for listed team members; no GPA or citizenship restriction is stated. Video deadline is Mar 30, 2026.

scholarships.name: Create Real Impact Contest

application_requirements.label: Register and submit a video or graphic design entry
application_requirements.details: Contest opens October 20, 2025 and closes April 15, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. ET; high school campaign schools register by March 30, 2026.

verification.notes: Official site: ages 14-22; legal residents of the 50 states/DC or DACA recipients; closes Apr 15, 2026 5 p.m. ET; no GPA cutoff found in published eligibility/FAQ.

scholarships.name: BigFuture Scholarships
scholarships.description: No-essay scholarship drawings for high school sophomores and juniors who complete BigFuture planning steps on their dashboard by class-year deadlines. Steps can qualify students for $500 and $40,000 awards.

application_requirements.details: Students qualify by completing planning steps on the BigFuture dashboard for their class year.

application_requirements.label: Complete class-year planning steps by published deadlines
application_requirements.details: Each completed step earns entries; earlier completion yields more chances to win per official rules.

verification.notes: Official College Board BigFuture page: no-essay; $500 and $40,000 drawings for sophomores/juniors; step deadlines vary by class year.

scholarships.name: Coca-Cola Leaders of Promise Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Coca-Cola Scholars Foundation (via Phi Theta Kappa)
scholarships.description: Phi Theta Kappa scholarship for newer members enrolled in associate-degree coursework who demonstrate leadership potential through PTK programs. Requires federal financial aid eligibility residency status.

application_requirements.label: Apply through the Phi Theta Kappa scholarship portal
application_requirements.details: Apply Now link on the official PTK Coca-Cola Leaders of Promise page when the cycle opens.

verification.notes: Official PTK page: up to $1,000; PTK member; 3.5 GPA; 12-36 credits by Jan 31 of application year; federal aid eligibility.

scholarships.name: All-USA Academic Team Scholarship

scholarships.description: Prestigious national honor for community college students nominated by their colleges for intellectual rigor, academic achievement, leadership, and civic engagement. Twenty national team members each receive a $5,000 scholarship.

application_requirements.details: Students are nominated through their college; more than 2,000 nominees compete nationally each year.

verification.notes: Official PTK news page: 20 members at $5,000 each; college nomination required; PTK competitive scholarship program.

scholarships.name: Coca-Cola Academic Team Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Coca-Cola Scholars Foundation (via Phi Theta Kappa)
scholarships.award_amount: up to 1,300 awards (Gold/Silver/Bronze tiers; amounts vary)
scholarships.description: Recognizes high-achieving community college students with academic excellence, leadership, and service. Requires college nomination for the All-USA Academic Team pathway and enrollment in associate-degree coursework.

application_requirements.label: Be nominated by your college for the All-USA Academic Team
application_requirements.details: Applicants must be nominated by their college; must not have been previously nominated for All-USA or Coca-Cola Academic Teams.

application_requirements.details: Apply through the sponsor's official page when the cycle opens.

scholarships.description: Joint ASA Social Statistics Section scholarship of up to $1,000 supporting students exploring careers in government statistics.

verification.notes: Official ASA awards hub: Social Statistics Section scholarship up to $1,000 for government statistics careers; deadline historically May 1 — est. 2027-05-01.

application_requirements.label: Complete the official online application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the sponsor's official page when the cycle opens.

scholarships.name: National Beta Club Scholarship

special_requirements.label: Must be an active National Beta (Beta Club) Senior member
special_requirements.details: Applicant must be a registered, active National Beta member with a valid membership ID number, registered with National Beta Headquarters by the application deadline; verified via member ID and last name at scholarship.betaclub.org.

scholarships.description: The National Beta Club awards over $300,000 annually to active 12th-grade Senior Beta Members, with 260 recipients receiving amounts ranging from $1,000 to $20,000 based on academic excellence, leadership, and service.

application_requirements.label: Verify active National Beta membership using member ID number and last name
application_requirements.details: Applicants must be high school seniors with an active National Beta membership ID to access the online application at scholarship.betaclub.org.

application_requirements.label: Submit Phase 1 application with GPA, test scores, and transcript
application_requirements.details: Includes contact information, unweighted GPA, ACT/SAT scores, high school transcript, National Beta event attendance, leadership roles/awards, service hours, and a resume of extracurriculars and honors.

application_requirements.label: Upload high school transcript and ACT/SAT score documentation
If selected as a top-100 semifinalist, submit Phase 2 materials
application_requirements.details: Includes one sponsor recommendation, one to three candidate evaluations, two student essays, and work history (if applicable), due by the semifinalist materials deadline.

scholarships.name: Terry Foundation Traditional (Freshman) Scholarship

special_requirements.label: Must apply through and be nominated by a Terry-affiliated Texas public university
special_requirements.details: Students cannot apply directly to the Terry Foundation. Application is submitted through one of 11 affiliated Texas public universities (UT Austin, Texas A&M, University of Houston, Texas State, UT Dallas, UT San Antonio, UT Arlington, UT El Paso, Texas Tech, University of North Texas, Texas Woman's University), which nominates candidates to the Foundation.

special_requirements.label: No direct application to the Terry Foundation is accepted
special_requirements.details: Applications, deadlines, and nomination processes are managed individually by each of the 11 affiliated universities, not by the Terry Foundation itself.

scholarships.description: The Terry Foundation awards full-ride, last-dollar scholarships to Texas high school graduates enrolling as full-time freshmen at one of 11 affiliated Texas public universities, based on academic achievement, leadership, and demonstrated financial need.

application_requirements.label: Graduate from an accredited Texas high school or homeschool
application_requirements.details: Applicants must not have taken a gap year immediately following graduation.

application_requirements.label: Apply to and gain admission to a Terry-affiliated Texas public university as a full-time entering freshman
application_requirements.details: Applications are submitted through the chosen affiliated university's own process and timeline, not directly to the Terry Foundation.

application_requirements.label: Complete and submit the FAFSA to demonstrate financial need
application_requirements.details: Must be submitted according to the deadlines specified by the nominating university.

application_requirements.label: Be nominated by the affiliated university
application_requirements.details: Campus partners submit nominee lists (approximately 26-90 candidates per school) to the Terry Foundation in mid-February.

application_requirements.label: Complete a required interview with the Terry Foundation Interview Panel
application_requirements.details: Interviews occur in late March/early April at panels held in El Paso, Dallas, or Houston; candidates must attend their assigned date.

application_requirements.label: Commit to residing on campus for the first year

scholarships.name: AFSA High School Senior Scholarship Contest

special_requirements.label: Family members of AFSA staff or national board members are ineligible
special_requirements.details: The official site states the contest is not open to family members of AFSA staff or national board members.

special_requirements.label: Winners chosen by random drawing weighted by quiz score
special_requirements.details: Applicants read a short passage about fire sprinklers and answer an 8-question quiz; each correct answer earns one entry into a random computer drawing for one of five $1,000 awards. This is not a merit-ranked competition.

scholarships.description: The AFSA High School Senior Scholarship Contest, run by the American Fire Sprinkler Association, is a no-essay scholarship contest in which eligible high school seniors read a short passage about fire sprinklers and answer an 8-question quiz for a chance to win one of five $1,000 scholarships, awarded by random drawing.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online entry form
application_requirements.details: Applicants fill out a brief online form on the AFSA scholarship site to begin the contest entry.

application_requirements.label: Read the provided passage about fire sprinklers and industry careers
application_requirements.details: Entrants review short educational content about fire sprinkler safety and careers before taking the quiz.

application_requirements.label: Answer the 8-question reading comprehension quiz
application_requirements.details: Each correct answer earns one entry into the random drawing for a $1,000 scholarship.

application_requirements.label: Submit only one entry
application_requirements.details: Multiple entries from the same applicant result in disqualification.

application_requirements.label: If selected as a winner, provide identity verification and school paperwork within 60 days
application_requirements.details: Winners must submit two forms of ID (e.g., birth certificate, U.S. passport, or green card) and complete required school paperwork within 60 days of contest close, or forfeit the award.

scholarships.name: NSHSS Nobel Academic Excellence Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: National Society of High School Scholars (NSHSS)

special_requirements.label: Must be a current NSHSS high school member
special_requirements.details: Open to NSHSS high school members graduating in 2026, 2027, 2028, or 2029; NSHSS membership itself has separate eligibility criteria and is required before applying.

scholarships.description: The NSHSS Nobel Academic Excellence Scholarship is a merit-based award from the National Society of High School Scholars (NSHSS), open to NSHSS high school members graduating 2026-2029, recognizing academic excellence, leadership, and positive impact on society; top winners receive $5,000.

application_requirements.label: Submit an educator recommendation
Write a 500-800 word prompt response
application_requirements.details: Prompt: how you are making a positive impact on society and how your college plans will help you continue that impact.

items.prompt: How are you already making a positive impact on society, and how do you hope to expand that impact in the future? Share your vision for creating change and how your college plans will help you achieve your goals.

application_requirements.label: Submit optional pictures/videos/media showing leadership
Provide a color headshot suitable for website posting
Submit an academic resume
Submit a current transcript
application_requirements.details: Official or unofficial transcript accepted.

scholarships.name: Davis-Putter Scholarship Fund

special_requirements.label: Must be an active participant in movements for progressive social change
special_requirements.details: Site states no application will be seriously considered without evidence of active engagement in social justice / progressive movement work (opposing capitalism, racism/white supremacy, hetero-patriarchy and gender-based violence, and environmental exploitation); aspiring to be involved is not sufficient.

scholarships.description: The Davis-Putter Scholarship Fund provides need-based grants to students who are active participants in movements for progressive social change, including work opposing capitalism, racism/white supremacy, hetero-patriarchy and gender-based violence, and environmental exploitation. Applicants must be enrolled in or have applied to a degree, certificate, or trade/technical program and demonstrate financial need.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online application form
application_requirements.details: Applications are available starting January 1 each year via the official Davis-Putter website.

application_requirements.label: Submit a personal statement describing movement involvement and financial need
application_requirements.details: The fund states no application will be seriously considered without evidence of active engagement in social justice/progressive movement work; aspiring to be involved is not sufficient.

application_requirements.label: Provide two letters of recommendation
Submit recent academic transcripts
application_requirements.details: Unofficial transcripts are acceptable.

application_requirements.label: Submit Student Aid Report (SAR) or equivalent
application_requirements.details: Applicants can submit a SAR, state equivalent, or complete the FAFSA Worksheet to the best of their ability; FAFSA itself is not required.

scholarships.name: Shari Simon Greenberg Community Scholarship

special_requirements.label: Must live near a Simon mall, Mills, or Premium Outlets community
special_requirements.details: Eligibility is limited to graduating high school seniors who reside in a community surrounding a Simon-owned mall, Mills, or Premium Outlet location; applications are submitted through the Simon Youth Foundation's designated portal (apply.mykaleidoscope.com).

scholarships.description: The Shari Simon Greenberg Community Scholarship (formerly the SYF Community Scholarship) is offered by the Simon Youth Foundation to graduating high school seniors living near a Simon mall or in surrounding communities, awarding one-time Community Awards (up to $1,500) or renewable Awards of Excellence based on academic promise, financial need, and other factors.

application_requirements.label: Confirm residency near a qualifying Simon mall/Mills/Premium Outlets community
application_requirements.details: Applicant must live in a community surrounding a Simon Property Group mall, Mills, or Premium Outlet location.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online scholarship application via the SYF application portal
application_requirements.details: Applications are submitted through apply.mykaleidoscope.com during the announced application window.

application_requirements.label: Submit written response(s)/essay as part of the application
application_requirements.details: Selection criteria explicitly include a 'written response' component alongside academic promise and financial need.

application_requirements.label: Demonstrate financial need
application_requirements.details: Financial need is one of the stated evaluation criteria; first-generation college students are given close consideration.

application_requirements.label: Plan to enroll at an accredited college, university, or vocational/technical school
application_requirements.details: Scholarship funds must be applied toward tuition at an accredited postsecondary institution.

scholarships.name: Gen and Kelly Tanabe Scholarship
scholarships.description: A merit-based scholarship funded by authors Gen and Kelly Tanabe, open to U.S.-resident high school (grades 9-12), college, and graduate students, with winners chosen primarily on their submitted personal statement.

application_requirements.label: Submit the official online application
application_requirements.details: Official FAQ states: "Only online applications will be accepted."

application_requirements.label: Write and submit a personal statement
application_requirements.details: Winners are chosen by committee based primarily on the submitted personal statement.

application_requirements.label: Confirm U.S. residency status
application_requirements.details: Applicants must be legal residents of the U.S.; studying abroad is permitted.

application_requirements.label: Be enrolled in grades 9-12, college, or graduate school (or a home-schooled/adult learner)

scholarships.name: American Welding Society (AWS) National Scholarships

special_requirements.label: AWS Membership required or preferred for some scholarships
special_requirements.details: FAQ states: "AWS Membership is required for some welding scholarships. For scholarships that do not require membership, selection committees typically prioritize applicants that are AWS Members."

scholarships.description: AWS Foundation National Scholarships are a portfolio of 130+ donor-funded awards for students pursuing a bachelor's or associate degree in welding or a related discipline, selected through a centralized online application portal.

application_requirements.label: Register or log in to the AWS scholarship application portal
Complete the Contact Information and Academic Profile sections
application_requirements.details: After these sections are complete, the portal displays all scholarships the applicant is eligible for.

application_requirements.label: Submit a Personal Statement essay (background, welding/work experience, career goals)
Provide an official transcript and two letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Letters must be from teachers, work supervisors, or mentors who can attest to work ethic and accomplishments.

application_requirements.label: Upload a summary of school costs (tuition, fees, books, supplies) and a headshot photo
For need-based awards, submit FAFSA Submission Summary; free AWS student membership encouraged
application_requirements.details: Some National Scholarships require demonstrated financial need (cost of attendance exceeding the Student Aid Index).

scholarships.name: Zonta Young Women in Leadership Award (formerly Young Women in Public Affairs Award)
scholarships.sponsor: Zonta International / Zonta Foundation for Women

special_requirements.label: Must apply through a local Zonta club (or e-club/district) rather than directly to Zonta International
special_requirements.details: Official page states: 'The application process must start with a Zonta club. All application materials must be received at a Zonta club by the deadline printed on the application.' Applicants in areas with no local club may apply via a Zonta e-club or directly to the district.

scholarships.description: The Zonta Young Women in Leadership Award recognizes women aged 16-19 for leadership skills and commitment to public service and civic causes, awarding US$5,000 to each of 37 international recipients selected from club- and district-level competitions.

application_requirements.label: Locate a Zonta club or e-club in your area using the Club Locator, or contact Zonta International Headquarters if no local club exists
application_requirements.details: Applicants from geographic areas within a Zonta district where no clubs are located may apply via a Zonta e-club or directly to the district.

application_requirements.label: Submit completed application directly to a Zonta club by the deadline printed on that club's application (not to Zonta International)
application_requirements.details: The international site states local club deadlines vary and are not published centrally; applicants must contact a club directly for deadline information.

application_requirements.label: Be a woman aged 16-19 as of April 1 of the award year, living in a Zonta district or a citizen of a Zonta country
Demonstrate active commitment to volunteerism, leadership experience, and knowledge of Zonta International
application_requirements.details: Leadership experience may be in government, student government, or the workplace.

application_requirements.label: Be selected by the local club, then advanced by the district (each district may submit 1-2 applicants) for international-level consideration
application_requirements.details: This step is performed by the club/district on the applicant's behalf, not a direct applicant action.

scholarships.name: NABA National Scholarship Program
scholarships.sponsor: National Association of Black Accountants, Inc. (NABA Foundation)

special_requirements.label: Must be an active NABA student member (free to join) per NABA Bylaws Section 2.2(b)
special_requirements.details: Official page requires applicants to 'Be an active NABA student member, as defined under NABA Bylaws, Section 2.2 (b)(i) and (ii)'; student membership is free.

scholarships.description: The NABA Foundation's National Scholarship Program awards over 150 scholarships annually, ranging from $2,500 to $10,000, to active NABA student members enrolled full-time at accredited U.S. colleges/universities pursuing accounting, finance, technology, or business-related degrees.

application_requirements.label: Become an active NABA student member at no cost
application_requirements.details: Required under NABA Bylaws Section 2.2(b) before a scholarship application can be considered.

application_requirements.label: Be currently enrolled full time at an accredited 2-year or 4-year U.S. college/university for the 2025-2026 academic year
Be a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident (green card holder); IRS 'resident alien' tax status does not qualify
Submit an application through the NABA scholarship portal ("Apply Here" link on the official scholarships page)
Demonstrate academic achievement, leadership, service, professional promise, and engagement with NABA's mission
application_requirements.details: Selection is merit-based and evaluated on these factors per the official page.

scholarships.name: Golden Door Scholars
scholarships.sponsor: Golden Door Scholars (a program of Road to Hire)

special_requirements.label: Must attend one of ~20 Golden Door Scholars partner colleges/universities
special_requirements.details: roadtohire.org/faq/gds states the scholarship 'funds a four-year bachelor's degree at a regional partner school only.' Partners listed include Davidson, Duke, Elon, Guilford, Meredith, Oglethorpe, Queens, Wingate, Bard (regional) and Case Western, Georgetown, Hamilton, Oberlin, Rhodes, Smith, Tufts, Belmont Abbey. Receiving the award does not guarantee admission to any one school.

special_requirements.label: Restricted to students ineligible for federal aid due to immigration status (undocumented/DACA/TPS)
special_requirements.details: The official domain's page title is 'Scholarship for Undocumented Students - Golden Door Scholars' and Road to Hire content describes bridging the financial-aid gap for undocumented students. This restriction does not map to any citizenship_requirement enum value, so that field is set to VERIFY and the restriction is captured here; exact eligibility wording should be confirmed by a reviewer in...

scholarships.description: Golden Door Scholars, a Road to Hire program, funds a four-year bachelor's degree exclusively at designated partner colleges for high-achieving students who cannot access federal financial aid due to immigration status, with a required unweighted high school GPA of 3.2 and a 3.0 GPA maintained each semester in college.

application_requirements.label: Submit the Golden Door Scholars online application during the open application window
application_requirements.details: As of this verification, roadtohire.org/golden-door-scholars states 'Applications for the Golden Door Scholarship are Closed'; no current-cycle open date/deadline was posted.

application_requirements.label: Submit high school transcripts (unofficial transcripts are accepted)
application_requirements.details: If official transcripts aren't ready, a placeholder document noting they will follow is accepted per the FAQ.

application_requirements.label: Optionally submit recommendation letters and/or standardized test scores
application_requirements.details: FAQ states these are optional, not required, for the application.

application_requirements.label: Complete an interview if selected as a finalist
application_requirements.details: Finalists are assessed on academic achievement, college readiness, community engagement, leadership, and resilience per the FAQ.

scholarships.name: Vegetarian Resource Group College Scholarship
scholarships.description: VRG awards one $10,000 scholarship, five $5,000 scholarships, and additional runner-up prizes to graduating U.S. high school seniors who have shown compassion, courage, and a strong commitment to promoting a vegan diet/lifestyle in their school or community; payment is made directly to a U.S.-based college.

application_requirements.label: Complete the personal information section (contact info, GPA, high school, and college plans)
Write an essay addressing all 16 required prompts about vegan/vegetarian advocacy and future goals

items.prompt: How you promoted veganism in your high school and/or community
Your successes
Your challenges
What you learned/what you would do different
How you expect to promote veganism in college and beyond
Your future goals
Your strengths
Your weaknesses
Your "perfect" life in five years
Why you should receive this scholarship
How you became vegetarian or vegan and why you are vegetarian or vegan
How you define vegetarian and vegan
Why vegetarianism and veganism is important to you
What else is important to you
What vegetarian foods you would recommend to a non-vegetarian
What restaurants you would recommend to a non-vegetarian

application_requirements.label: Submit a copy of transcripts or report cards for the past two years
Submit three or more recommendation letters, including at least one from a teacher
Submit documentation of vegetarian/vegan promotion activities (photos, news clippings, etc.)
application_requirements.details: Listed among the requested application materials on the official page.

scholarships.name: Elizabeth Greenshields Foundation Grant

special_requirements.label: Restricted to representational/figurative artists in painting, sculpture, printmaking, or drawing, aged 18-41 at...
special_requirements.details: Eligibility uses an age band (18-41 at first application; former recipients may reapply beyond 41) and a 'student or emerging artist' career stage. Disciplines are limited to representational/figurative painting, sculpture, printmaking, and drawing; abstract, photography, digital, video, installation, and craft are excluded. No geographic, citizenship, or residency requirement.

scholarships.description: A grant from the Elizabeth Greenshields Foundation supporting young representational/figurative artists worldwide who are studying or working in painting, sculpture, printmaking, or drawing, to fund studio practice, exhibitions, residencies, or fine-arts/studio-arts education. Grants of CAD $17,000 (first grant) to CAD $20,000 (second/third grants) may be awarded to an artist up to three times in their lifetime.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online Eligibility Questionnaire before starting an application
application_requirements.details: Applicants must first complete an Eligibility Questionnaire on the application portal to confirm they meet all eligibility criteria before an application can be submitted.

application_requirements.label: Submit personal info, grant request description, artist statement, artistic proposal, and budget
application_requirements.details: The online application consists of seven mandatory tasks, including personal information/professional history, description of grant request, artist statement, artistic proposal, and budget.

application_requirements.label: Submit a portfolio of six representative works from the last two years plus one installation view
application_requirements.details: Images of six representative works produced by the applicant alone within the two years preceding the application, plus an installation view of one work in the portfolio, are required.

application_requirements.label: Have a referee submit a letter of recommendation directly through the application site
application_requirements.details: A letter of recommendation from a teacher, professor, artist, or curator familiar with the applicant and their work must be submitted directly through the application portal by the referee.

application_requirements.label: Apply online before the current application cycle's close date
application_requirements.details: Applications must be submitted through the official online portal before the cycle close date; applications by email or physical mail are not accepted. Grant funds may only be used for future expenses.

scholarships.name: KASF Scholarships

special_requirements.label: Korean heritage requirement
special_requirements.details: Must be of Korean heritage (Korean parent, grandparent, or great-grandparent, or adopted from Korea); non-Korean students may qualify only if extra funds remain available in a given region.

special_requirements.label: Must apply through the correct regional chapter
special_requirements.details: KASF is organized into 7 regional chapters (Eastern, Midwestern, Mountain States, Northeastern, Southern, Southwestern, Western). Applicants must apply to the region where their school is located; applications submitted to the wrong region are disqualified.

special_requirements.label: Korean War veteran descendant track (select regions)
special_requirements.details: Descendants of American veterans who served in the Korean War (June 25, 1950 - January 31, 1955) may qualify for a dedicated scholarship track, available only in select regions.

scholarships.description: KASF is a national nonprofit, volunteer-managed foundation that awards need- and merit-based scholarships (generally $500-$5,000) to students of Korean heritage enrolled full-time at U.S. schools, administered through seven regional chapters.

application_requirements.label: Determine and apply through the correct KASF regional chapter
application_requirements.details: Applicants must apply to the region where their school is/will be located during the scholarship year; applications submitted to the wrong region are disqualified.

application_requirements.label: Confirm Korean heritage, full-time enrollment, and GPA eligibility
application_requirements.details: Must be of Korean heritage, enrolled full-time during the scholarship application year, and hold a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0.

application_requirements.label: Submit online application by June 30 at 12:00am EST
application_requirements.details: The application deadline each cycle is June 30 at 12am EST; a 7-day grace period applies for supporting documents only.

application_requirements.label: Submit required essay
application_requirements.details: Essay is one of the evaluation criteria alongside financial need, scholastic achievement, recommendations, and community service/extracurricular activities.

application_requirements.label: Provide letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Recommenders are emailed a link to an online form, or may write and upload a letter.

application_requirements.label: Submit financial documentation
application_requirements.details: International/self-supporting students submit a Personal Financial Statement form; other applicants submit tax documents demonstrating financial need.

scholarships.name: Sons of Norway Foundation Scholarships

special_requirements.label: Membership required for some (not all) of the 17 funds
special_requirements.details: Some of the seventeen scholarship funds require Sons of Norway membership; others are open to non-members. An online eligibility quiz determines which funds an applicant qualifies for.

special_requirements.label: Connection to Sons of Norway / Norwegian heritage emphasized
special_requirements.details: The required essay must address the applicant's 'connection to Sons of Norway.' Individual funds (e.g., King Olav V Norwegian-American Heritage Scholarship) may carry specific ancestry requirements not detailed at the program-overview level.

scholarships.description: The Sons of Norway Foundation administers seventeen distinct scholarship funds supporting students pursuing education in the United States, Canada, and Norway (including Norwegian folk high schools), open to both Sons of Norway members and non-members depending on the specific fund.

application_requirements.label: Complete the eligibility quiz to identify qualifying scholarship funds
application_requirements.details: An online eligibility quiz on the Foundation's site determines which of the 17 scholarship funds an applicant qualifies for.

application_requirements.label: Submit application through the grants and scholarship portal by January 15
application_requirements.details: The application must be complete and submitted on or before January 15 each cycle; the portal allows saving drafts.

application_requirements.label: Write the required essay
application_requirements.details: Essay must address why the applicant is applying and their connection to Sons of Norway.

items.prompt: Explain why you are applying and describe your connection to Sons of Norway.

application_requirements.label: Provide two letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Applicant must supply email addresses for two recommenders; letters do not need to be complete by the January 15 deadline but should follow soon after.

application_requirements.label: List academic institutions applied to or accepted by
application_requirements.details: If the applicant has not decided on a school, they should list the schools applied to and note any acceptances.

scholarships.name: National Leadership Grants (Sons of Italy Foundation Scholarship Program)
scholarships.sponsor: Sons of Italy Foundation (SIF), the philanthropic foundation of the Order Sons and Daughters of Italy in America (OSDIA/OSIA)

special_requirements.label: Italian ancestry required (parent or grandparent)
special_requirements.details: Applicant must trace Italian ancestry to at least one parent or grandparent. Open to U.S. citizens or green card holders only. Prior SIF National Leadership Grant recipients are ineligible to reapply.

special_requirements.label: $35 application fee includes one-year OSDIA At-Large membership
special_requirements.details: A non-refundable $35 fee is required to process the application; the fee includes a one-year At-Large membership in OSDIA.

scholarships.description: National Leadership Grants are merit-based scholarships awarded annually by the Sons of Italy Foundation to students of Italian descent enrolled in a four-year undergraduate, graduate, or professional program at an accredited U.S. institution.

application_requirements.label: Register and create an online application account
Pay the $35 non-refundable application fee
application_requirements.details: Fee includes a one-year At-Large OSDIA membership.

application_requirements.label: Submit unofficial transcripts
application_requirements.details: Transcripts through the most recent completed term are required.

application_requirements.label: Submit 2 letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Letters must be from professional contacts, not family or friends.

application_requirements.label: Submit required essay(s)
application_requirements.details: Essays are a mandatory evaluation component; Italian-language majors may submit one essay in Italian. AI-generated content results in disqualification.

application_requirements.label: Submit official standardized test scores from the past 5 years
application_requirements.details: E.g., ACT, SAT, GMAT, GRE, LSAT, MCAT, as applicable.

scholarships.name: SME Education Foundation Scholarships
scholarships.description: The SME Education Foundation offers a single unified application covering more than 60 distinct merit- and need-based scholarship programs for graduating high school seniors and current college students pursuing associate or bachelor's degrees in technical fields that directly support manufacturing.

application_requirements.label: Register and create an account at the application portal
application_requirements.details: Portal: scholarships.smeef.org.

application_requirements.label: Submit one unified application
application_requirements.details: Applicants are automatically considered for every scholarship they are eligible for across 60+ programs.

application_requirements.label: Answer short-answer questions on educational/career objectives
application_requirements.details: Includes explaining educational and career objectives related to manufacturing, motivation, and how the scholarship will help.

items.prompt: Please explain your educational and career objectives as it relates to manufacturing?
What is your motivation in pursuing a career in manufacturing?
If awarded how will an SME Education Foundation Scholarship enable you to pursue a career in manufacturing?

application_requirements.label: Submit transcripts
Submit a list of completed STEM courses
Provide two letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: One reference must be a faculty member.

scholarships.name: AIST Foundation Steel Intern Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: AIST Foundation (Association for Iron & Steel Technology)

special_requirements.label: Requires paid summer internship/co-op at a North American steel company
special_requirements.details: Recipients must complete a paid summer internship or co-op at a North American steel company as part of the award, must be available and willing to relocate for the placement, and must maintain a 2.5+ GPA plus a satisfactory internship performance review to retain the award.

scholarships.description: The AIST Foundation Steel Intern Scholarship provides up to 50 one-year scholarships (plus a top Premier Intern Scholarship) paired with a paid summer internship or co-op at a North American steel company, for undergraduates in engineering, computer science, data science, environmental/sustainability science, or industrial safety fields pursuing careers in the steel industry.

application_requirements.label: Submit resume in PDF format
Submit essay (max 2 pages)
application_requirements.details: Essay must cite professional goals and explain interest in a steel-industry career.

items.prompt: Describe your professional goals, explain why you are interested in a career in the steel industry, and discuss how your skills could be applied to enhance the industry.
items.length: not to exceed two pages in length

application_requirements.label: Obtain 1-5 letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Letters are sent directly to the program contact.

application_requirements.label: Submit academic transcript
Complete the online application by the deadline
application_requirements.details: Deadline: 5 October 2026 for 2027-2028 awards.

scholarships.name: WAI Scholarship Program (WAI2027 Scholarships)

special_requirements.label: Active WAI membership required to apply
special_requirements.details: Applicants must be current WAI members by August 30, 2026, or hold a membership with an expiration date of March 30, 2027 or later, to apply for the WAI2027 scholarship cycle.

special_requirements.label: Program intended for women in aviation/aerospace
special_requirements.details: WAI is a women-in-aviation organization; its scholarship program is intended for women pursuing aviation/aerospace careers, though the FAQ states WAI does not consider gender in judging individual awards.

scholarships.description: WAI's annual scholarship program is a large umbrella portfolio of dozens of individually named scholarships covering flight training and aviation/aerospace education costs, open only to current WAI members. Award amounts, GPA minimums, and citizenship rules vary by individual named scholarship within the portfolio rather than being fixed program-wide.

application_requirements.label: Obtain or maintain active WAI membership
application_requirements.details: Must be a WAI member by August 30, 2026, or hold membership expiring March 30, 2027 or later.

application_requirements.label: Submit application through the WAI online scholarship portal
application_requirements.details: Applications open June 1, 2026 and close September 1, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET; only online portal submissions are accepted.

application_requirements.label: Submit a 500-word essay
application_requirements.details: Essay must address what inspired the applicant's aviation/aerospace career interest, community involvement, and how the scholarship helps achieve goals.

items.prompt: Describe what inspired you to join the aviation or aerospace industry, what keeps you motivated in this field, your contributions to the community, and how this scholarship will change your life.

application_requirements.label: Submit a resume
application_requirements.details: Maximum two pages, no photos.

application_requirements.label: Provide letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Two letters (max one page each) for general scholarships, or one letter for Harvard-track scholarships; cannot be from family members. Applicant is responsible for uploading them.

scholarships.name: EAA Aviation Scholarships (Flight Training & Post-Secondary Scholarships)
scholarships.sponsor: EAA (Experimental Aircraft Association) / EAA Aviation Foundation
scholarships.description: EAA runs a large annual portfolio of 80+ individually named Flight Training and Post-Secondary aviation scholarships (plus the separately-administered Ray Aviation Scholarship for youth flight training via EAA chapters) through a single online application window each fall. Amounts are set by individual donor funding and are not finalized until near the award date, so no fixed dollar figure applies program-wide.

application_requirements.label: Confirm age and citizenship/residency eligibility
application_requirements.details: Must be at least 16 years old by the March 2 award date; must be a US/Canada citizen or resident, or otherwise eligible to study/work in the US or Canada (e.g. via visa), with proof of eligibility required for international applicants.

application_requirements.label: Submit application via the EAA online scholarship portal
application_requirements.details: Applications open October 1 and close December 19 each year; late or incomplete applications are not accepted, no exceptions.

application_requirements.label: Complete essay/short-answer responses
application_requirements.details: All essays and responses must be the applicant's own original work; AI-generated content exceeding 25% will disqualify the application.

application_requirements.label: Provide a character/reference contact
application_requirements.details: Reference receives a link to complete a recommendation as part of the application.

application_requirements.label: Submit transcript with unweighted GPA (post-secondary applicants)
application_requirements.details: Post-secondary applicants must report an unweighted 4.0-scale GPA; a minimum 2.5 GPA is required for post-secondary scholarships (not stated as required for flight-training-only applicants).

scholarships.name: AFSA National High School Essay Contest

special_requirements.label: 1,000-1,500 word essay on assigned foreign policy topic
special_requirements.details: Essay must be 1,000-1,500 words (excluding sources), double-spaced, 12-pt font, one-inch margins, MLA style, citing at least three primary sources; general encyclopedias like Wikipedia are not acceptable. Previous first-place winners and immediate relatives of AFSA/NSLC/Semester at Sea staff are ineligible.

scholarships.description: The AFSA National High School Essay Contest, sponsored by the American Foreign Service Association, invites U.S. students in grades 9-12 to write an essay on a current U.S. foreign policy and diplomacy topic. The first-place winner receives $2,500, an all-expenses-paid trip to Washington, D.C., and a Semester at Sea scholarship; the runner-up receives $1,250 and a National Student Leadership Conference scholarship.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online registration form
application_requirements.details: All fields are mandatory and the registration form must include a teacher or sponsor name.

application_requirements.label: Write a 1,000-1,500 word essay on the assigned foreign policy topic
application_requirements.details: Word count excludes the source list. Essay must argue one of two specified positions on the current year's topic.

items.prompt: The Fragile Front Line: U.S. Diplomacy and the Future of Soft Power. In 2025, those tools took a significant hit. The U.S. Agency for International Development was dismantled; the U.S. Agency for Global Media (home to Voice of America) was effectively closed; most exchange programs, including Fulbright, were suspended; and the Foreign Service itself was cut by about 25 percent. How can the United States build and sustain relationships without these efforts? In 1,000 to 1,500 words, make a case for one of two positions: either explain how the United States can maintain global leadership without these tools, or argue that it cannot—and why. In either direction, focus on the role of the U.S. Foreign Service with fewer people and fewer tools. If you believe leadership can be sustained, lay out clear, realistic steps diplomats could take to achieve their goals. If you believe it cannot, show where capacity would fall short, which risks would rise, and what costs would follow.

application_requirements.label: Support the essay with at least three primary sources and a bibliography
application_requirements.details: Sources should include academic journals, news magazines, newspapers, books, government documents, and research publications; general encyclopedias (e.g. Wikipedia) are not acceptable.

application_requirements.label: Format the essay per contest guidelines
application_requirements.details: Double-spaced, 12-point font, one-inch margins, following MLA Handbook style with a bibliography; do not include the student's or school's name on the essay pages.

application_requirements.label: Upload the essay as a Word document via the official submission portal by the deadline
application_requirements.details: Submit at afsascholarships.communityforce.com by March 1 at 11:59 p.m. EDT; late submissions are automatically disqualified.
